017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.Visibility.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.SystemUtils.*;
028import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
029
030import java.beans.*;
031import java.io.*;
032import java.lang.reflect.*;
033import java.math.*;
034import java.net.*;
035import java.nio.charset.*;
036import java.time.*;
037import java.time.temporal.*;
038import java.util.*;
039import java.util.stream.*;
040
041import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.commons.conversion.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
048import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
049import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
050import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.Visibility;
051import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
052import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.*;
053import org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.*;
054import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
055import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
056import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
057import org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils.*;
058
059/**
060 * Bean context.
061 *
062 * <p>
063 * This class servers multiple purposes:
064 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
065 *    <li>
066 *       Provides the ability to wrap beans inside {@link Map} interfaces.
067 *    <li>
068 *       Serves as a repository for metadata on POJOs, such as associated {@link Bean @Marshalled} annotations,
069 *       {@link PropertyNamer PropertyNamers}, etc...  which are used to tailor how POJOs are serialized and parsed.
070 * </ul>
071 *
072 * <p>
073 * All serializers and parsers use this context so that they can handle POJOs using a common framework.
074 *
075 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean Contexts</h5>
076 *
077 * <p>
078 * Bean contexts are created through the {@link MarshallingContext#create() MarshallingContext.create()} and {@link Builder#build()} methods.
079 * <br>These context objects are read-only, reusable, and thread-safe.
080 *
081 * <p>
082 * Each bean context maintains a cache of {@link ClassMeta} objects that describe information about classes encountered.
083 * These <c>ClassMeta</c> objects are time-consuming to construct.
084 * Therefore, instances of {@link MarshallingContext} that share the same <js>"MarshallingContext.*"</js> property values share
085 * the same cache.  This allows for efficient reuse of <c>ClassMeta</c> objects so that the information about
086 * classes only needs to be calculated once.
087 * Because of this, many of the properties defined on the {@link MarshallingContext} class cannot be overridden on the session.
088 *
089 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean Sessions</h5>
090 *
091 * <p>
092 * Whereas <c>MarshallingContext</c> objects are permanent, unchangeable, cached, and thread-safe,
093 * {@link MarshallingSession} objects are ephemeral and not thread-safe.
094 * They are meant to be used as quickly-constructed scratchpads for creating bean maps.
095 * {@link BeanMap} objects can only be created through the session.
096 *
097 * <h5 class='topic'>MarshallingContext configuration properties</h5>
098 *
099 * <p>
100 * <c>BeanContexts</c> have several configuration properties that can be used to tweak behavior on how beans are
101 * handled.  These are denoted as the static <jsf>BEAN_*</jsf> fields on this class.
102 *
103 * <p>
104 * Some settings (e.g. {@link Builder#beansRequireDefaultConstructor()}) are used to differentiate between bean
105 * and non-bean classes.
106 * Attempting to create a bean map around one of these objects will throw a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
107 * The purpose for this behavior is so that the serializers can identify these non-bean classes and convert them to
108 * plain strings using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
109 *
110 * <p>
111 * Some settings (e.g. {@link Builder#beanFieldVisibility(Visibility)}) are used to determine what kinds of properties are
112 * detected on beans.
113 *
114 * <p>
115 * Some settings (e.g. {@link Builder#beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()}) change the runtime behavior of bean maps.
116 *
117 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
118 *
119 * <p class='bjava'>
120 *    <jc>// Construct a context from scratch.</jc>
121 *    MarshallingContext <jv>marshallingContext</jv> = MarshallingContext
122 *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
123 *       .beansRequireDefaultConstructor()
124 *       .notBeanClasses(Foo.<jk>class</jk>)
125 *       .build();
126 * </p>
127 *
128 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean Maps</h5>
129 *
130 * <p>
131 * {@link BeanMap BeanMaps} are wrappers around Java beans that allow properties to be retrieved and
132 * set using the common {@link Map#put(Object,Object)} and {@link Map#get(Object)} methods.
133 *
134 * <p>
135 * Bean maps are created in two ways...
136 * <ol>
137 *    <li>{@link MarshallingSession#toBeanMap(Object) MarshallingSession.toBeanMap()} - Wraps an existing bean inside a {@code Map}
138 *       wrapper.
139 *    <li>{@link MarshallingSession#newBeanMap(Class) MarshallingSession.newBeanMap()} - Create a new bean instance wrapped in a
140 *       {@code Map} wrapper.
141 * </ol>
142 *
143 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
144 *
145 * <p class='bjava'>
146 *    <jc>// A sample bean class</jc>
147 *    <jk>public class</jk> Person {
148 *       <jk>public</jk> String getName();
149 *       <jk>public void</jk> setName(String <jv>name</jv>);
150 *       <jk>public int</jk> getAge();
151 *       <jk>public void</jk> setAge(<jk>int</jk> <jv>age</jv>);
152 *    }
153 *
154 *    <jc>// Create a new bean session</jc>
155 *    MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv> = MarshallingContext.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.createSession();
156 *
157 *    <jc>// Wrap an existing bean in a new bean map</jc>
158 *    BeanMap&lt;Person&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = <jv>session</jv>.toBeanMap(<jk>new</jk> Person());
159 *    <jv>map1</jv>.put(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"John Smith"</js>);
160 *    <jv>map1</jv>.put(<js>"age"</js>, 45);
161 *
162 *    <jc>// Create a new bean instance wrapped in a new bean map</jc>
163 *    BeanMap&lt;Person&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = <jv>session</jv>.newBeanMap(Person.<jk>class</jk>);
164 *    <jv>map2</jv>.put(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"John Smith"</js>);
165 *    <jv>map2</jv>.put(<js>"age"</js>, 45);
166 *    Person <jv>person</jv> = <jv>map2</jv>.getBean();  <jc>// Get the bean instance that was created.</jc>
167 * </p>
168 *
169 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
170 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
171 * </ul>
172 *
173 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
174 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/BeanContexts">Bean Context Basics</a>
175 * </ul>
176 */
177@SuppressWarnings({
178   "unchecked",  // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
179   "rawtypes",   // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
180   "java:S6539", // Collection.toArray() usage intentional
181   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
182   "java:S1452", // Wildcard required - ClassMeta<?> for parameter resolution and type variables
183   "java:S1612"  // Lambdas used instead of method references for readability in complex chained expressions
184})
185public class MarshallingContext extends Context implements ConversionFinder, BeanTypeResolver {
186
187   // Property name constants
188   private static final String PROP_beanClassVisibility = "beanClassVisibility";
189   private static final String PROP_beanConstructorVisibility = "beanConstructorVisibility";
190   private static final String PROP_beanDictionary = "beanDictionary";
191   private static final String PROP_beanFieldVisibility = "beanFieldVisibility";
192   private static final String PROP_beanMethodVisibility = "beanMethodVisibility";
193   private static final String PROP_beansRequireDefaultConstructor = "beansRequireDefaultConstructor";
194   private static final String PROP_beansRequireSerializable = "beansRequireSerializable";
195   private static final String PROP_beansRequireSettersForGetters = "beansRequireSettersForGetters";
196   private static final String PROP_beansRequireSomeProperties = "beansRequireSomeProperties";
197   private static final String PROP_id = "id";
198   private static final String PROP_ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters = "ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters";
199   private static final String PROP_ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters = "ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters";
200   private static final String PROP_ignoreTransientFields = "ignoreTransientFields";
201   private static final String PROP_ignoreUnknownBeanProperties = "ignoreUnknownBeanProperties";
202   private static final String PROP_ignoreUnknownNullBeanProperties = "ignoreUnknownNullBeanProperties";
203   private static final String PROP_notBeanClasses = "notBeanClasses";
204   private static final String PROP_notBeanPackageNames = "notBeanPackageNames";
205   private static final String PROP_notBeanPackagePrefixes = "notBeanPackagePrefixes";
206   private static final String PROP_unsortedProperties = "unsortedProperties";
207   private static final String PROP_swaps = "swaps";
208   private static final String PROP_durationFormat = "durationFormat";
209   private static final String PROP_periodFormat = "periodFormat";
210   private static final String PROP_calendarFormat = "calendarFormat";
211   private static final String PROP_dateFormat = "dateFormat";
212   private static final String PROP_temporalFormat = "temporalFormat";
213   private static final String PROP_timeZoneFormat = "timeZoneFormat";
214   private static final String PROP_localeFormat = "localeFormat";
215   private static final String PROP_binaryFormat = "binaryFormat";
216   private static final String PROP_enumFormat = "enumFormat";
217   private static final String PROP_uuidFormat = "uuidFormat";
218   private static final String PROP_bitSetFormat = "bitSetFormat";
219   private static final String PROP_bigNumberFormat = "bigNumberFormat";
220   private static final String PROP_booleanFormat = "booleanFormat";
221   private static final String PROP_floatFormat = "floatFormat";
222   private static final String PROP_currencyFormat = "currencyFormat";
223   private static final String PROP_classFormat = "classFormat";
224   private static final String PROP_classLoader = "classLoader";
225   private static final String PROP_useInterfaceProxies = "useInterfaceProxies";
226   private static final String PROP_useJavaBeanIntrospector = "useJavaBeanIntrospector";
227   private static final String PROP_validateSchema = "validateSchema";
228
229   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
230   private static final String ARG_bc = "bc";
231   private static final String ARG_beanClass = "beanClass";
232   private static final String ARG_beanClassName = "beanClassName";
233   private static final String ARG_c = "c";
234   private static final String ARG_implClass = "implClass";
235   private static final String ARG_interfaceClass = "interfaceClass";
236   private static final String ARG_normalClass = "normalClass";
237   private static final String ARG_object = "object";
238   private static final String ARG_o = "o";
239   private static final String ARG_on = "on";
240   private static final String ARG_pojoClass = "pojoClass";
241   private static final String ARG_properties = "properties";
242   private static final String ARG_swapFunction = "swapFunction";
243   private static final String ARG_swappedClass = "swappedClass";
244   private static final String ARG_unswapFunction = "unswapFunction";
245   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
246   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
247
248   /**
249    * Builder class.
250    */
251   public static class Builder extends Context.Builder<Builder> {
252
253      private static final Cache<HashKey,MarshallingContext> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, MarshallingContext.class).build();
254
255      private static int integer(boolean...values) {
256         var n = 0;
257         for (var b : values)
258            n = (n << 1) | (b ? 1 : 0);
259         return n;
260      }
261      private Visibility beanClassVisibility;
262      private Visibility beanConstructorVisibility;
263      private Visibility beanMethodVisibility;
264      private Visibility beanFieldVisibility;
265      private boolean disableBeansRequireSomeProperties;
266      private boolean beanMapPutReturnsOldValue;
267      private boolean beansRequireDefaultConstructor;
268      private boolean beansRequireSerializable;
269      private boolean beansRequireSettersForGetters;
270      private boolean disableIgnoreTransientFields;
271      private boolean disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties;
272      private boolean disableIgnoreMissingSetters;
273      private boolean disableInterfaceProxies;
274      private boolean findFluentSetters;
275      private boolean ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters;
276      private boolean ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters;
277      private boolean ignoreUnknownBeanProperties;
278      private boolean ignoreUnknownEnumValues;
279      private boolean unsortedProperties;
280      private boolean useJavaBeanIntrospector;
281      private boolean validateSchema;
282      private String typePropertyName;
283      private MediaType mediaType;
284      private Locale locale;
285      private TimeZone timeZone;
286      private DurationFormat durationFormat;
287      private PeriodFormat periodFormat;
288      private CalendarFormat calendarFormat;
289      private DateFormat dateFormat;
290      private TemporalFormat temporalFormat;
291      private TimeZoneFormat timeZoneFormat;
292      private LocaleFormat localeFormat;
293      private BinaryFormat binaryFormat;
294      private EnumFormat enumFormat;
295      private UuidFormat uuidFormat;
296      private BitSetFormat bitSetFormat;
297      private BigNumberFormat bigNumberFormat;
298      private BooleanFormat booleanFormat;
299      private FloatFormat floatFormat;
300      private CurrencyFormat currencyFormat;
301      private ClassFormat classFormat;
302      private ClassLoader classLoader;
303      private Class<? extends PropertyNamer> propertyNamer;
304      private List<ClassInfo> beanDictionary;
305      private List<Object> swaps;
306      private Set<ClassInfo> notBeanClasses;
307      private Set<String> notBeanPackages;
308      private BeanStore beanStore;
309      private String activeView;
310      private boolean disableDefaultViewInclusion;
311
312      /**
313       * Constructor.
314       *
315       * All default settings.
316       */
317      protected Builder() {
318         beanClassVisibility = env("MarshallingContext.beanClassVisibility", PUBLIC);
319         beanConstructorVisibility = env("MarshallingContext.beanConstructorVisibility", PUBLIC);
320         beanDictionary = list();
321         beanFieldVisibility = env("MarshallingContext.beanFieldVisibility", PUBLIC);
322         beanMapPutReturnsOldValue = env("MarshallingContext.beanMapPutReturnsOldValue", false);
323         beanMethodVisibility = env("MarshallingContext.beanMethodVisibility", PUBLIC);
324         beansRequireDefaultConstructor = env("MarshallingContext.beansRequireDefaultConstructor", false);
325         beansRequireSerializable = env("MarshallingContext.beansRequireSerializable", false);
326         beansRequireSettersForGetters = env("MarshallingContext.beansRequireSettersForGetters", false);
327         disableBeansRequireSomeProperties = env("MarshallingContext.disableBeansRequireSomeProperties", false);
328         disableIgnoreMissingSetters = env("MarshallingContext.disableIgnoreMissingSetters", false);
329         disableIgnoreTransientFields = env("MarshallingContext.disableIgnoreTransientFields", false);
330         disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties = env("MarshallingContext.disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties", false);
331         disableInterfaceProxies = env("MarshallingContext.disableInterfaceProxies", false);
332         findFluentSetters = env("MarshallingContext.findFluentSetters", false);
333         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters = env("MarshallingContext.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters", false);
334         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters = env("MarshallingContext.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters", false);
335         ignoreUnknownBeanProperties = env("MarshallingContext.ignoreUnknownBeanProperties", false);
336         ignoreUnknownEnumValues = env("MarshallingContext.ignoreUnknownEnumValues", false);
337         locale = env("MarshallingContext.locale").map(Locale::forLanguageTag).orElse(Locale.getDefault());
338         mediaType = env("MarshallingContext.mediaType").map(MediaType::of).orElse(null);
339         notBeanClasses = new TreeSet<>();
340         notBeanPackages = new TreeSet<>();
341         propertyNamer = null;
342         unsortedProperties = env("MarshallingContext.unsortedProperties", false);
343         swaps = list();
344         timeZone = env("MarshallingContext.timeZone").map(TimeZone::getTimeZone).orElse(null);
345         durationFormat = env("MarshallingContext.durationFormat", DurationFormat.ISO_8601_WITH_DAYS);
346         periodFormat = env("MarshallingContext.periodFormat", PeriodFormat.ISO_8601);
347         calendarFormat = env("MarshallingContext.calendarFormat", CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME);
348         dateFormat = env("MarshallingContext.dateFormat", DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME);
349         temporalFormat = env("MarshallingContext.temporalFormat", TemporalFormat.DEFAULT);
350         timeZoneFormat = env("MarshallingContext.timeZoneFormat", TimeZoneFormat.ID);
351         localeFormat = env("MarshallingContext.localeFormat", LocaleFormat.BCP_47);
352         binaryFormat = env("MarshallingContext.binaryFormat", BinaryFormat.NOT_SET);
353         enumFormat = env("MarshallingContext.enumFormat", EnumFormat.TO_STRING);
354         uuidFormat = env("MarshallingContext.uuidFormat", UuidFormat.STANDARD);
355         bitSetFormat = env("MarshallingContext.bitSetFormat", BitSetFormat.INDICES);
356         bigNumberFormat = env("MarshallingContext.bigNumberFormat", BigNumberFormat.NUMBER);
357         booleanFormat = env("MarshallingContext.booleanFormat", BooleanFormat.TRUE_FALSE);
358         floatFormat = env("MarshallingContext.floatFormat", FloatFormat.NaN_AS_NULL);
359         currencyFormat = env("MarshallingContext.currencyFormat", CurrencyFormat.ISO_CODE);
360         classFormat = env("MarshallingContext.classFormat", ClassFormat.FQCN);
361         typePropertyName = env("MarshallingContext.typePropertyName", "_type");
362         useJavaBeanIntrospector = env("MarshallingContext.useJavaBeanIntrospector", false);
363         validateSchema = env("MarshallingContext.validateSchema", false);
364         beanStore = null;
365      }
366
367      /**
368       * Copy constructor.
369       *
370       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
371       */
372      protected Builder(MarshallingContext copyFrom) {
373         super(copyFrom);
374         beanClassVisibility = copyFrom.beanClassVisibility;
375         beanConstructorVisibility = copyFrom.beanConstructorVisibility;
376         beanDictionary = copyOf(copyFrom.beanDictionary);
377         beanFieldVisibility = copyFrom.beanFieldVisibility;
378         beanMapPutReturnsOldValue = copyFrom.beanMapPutReturnsOldValue;
379         beanMethodVisibility = copyFrom.beanMethodVisibility;
380         beansRequireDefaultConstructor = copyFrom.beansRequireDefaultConstructor;
381         beansRequireSerializable = copyFrom.beansRequireSerializable;
382         beansRequireSettersForGetters = copyFrom.beansRequireSettersForGetters;
383         disableBeansRequireSomeProperties = ! copyFrom.beansRequireSomeProperties;
384         disableIgnoreMissingSetters = ! copyFrom.ignoreMissingSetters;
385         disableIgnoreTransientFields = ! copyFrom.ignoreTransientFields;
386         disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties = ! copyFrom.ignoreUnknownNullBeanProperties;
387         disableInterfaceProxies = ! copyFrom.useInterfaceProxies;
388         findFluentSetters = copyFrom.findFluentSetters;
389         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters = copyFrom.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters;
390         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters = copyFrom.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters;
391         ignoreUnknownBeanProperties = copyFrom.ignoreUnknownBeanProperties;
392         ignoreUnknownEnumValues = copyFrom.ignoreUnknownEnumValues;
393         locale = copyFrom.locale;
394         mediaType = copyFrom.mediaType;
395         notBeanClasses = new TreeSet<>(copyFrom.notBeanClasses);
396         notBeanPackages = toSortedSet(copyFrom.notBeanPackages, false);
397         propertyNamer = copyFrom.propertyNamer;
398         unsortedProperties = copyFrom.unsortedProperties;
399         swaps = copyOf(copyFrom.swaps);
400         timeZone = copyFrom.timeZone;
401         durationFormat = copyFrom.durationFormat;
402         periodFormat = copyFrom.periodFormat;
403         calendarFormat = copyFrom.calendarFormat;
404         dateFormat = copyFrom.dateFormat;
405         temporalFormat = copyFrom.temporalFormat;
406         timeZoneFormat = copyFrom.timeZoneFormat;
407         localeFormat = copyFrom.localeFormat;
408         binaryFormat = copyFrom.binaryFormat;
409         enumFormat = copyFrom.enumFormat;
410         uuidFormat = copyFrom.uuidFormat;
411         bitSetFormat = copyFrom.bitSetFormat;
412         bigNumberFormat = copyFrom.bigNumberFormat;
413         booleanFormat = copyFrom.booleanFormat;
414         floatFormat = copyFrom.floatFormat;
415         currencyFormat = copyFrom.currencyFormat;
416         classFormat = copyFrom.classFormat;
417         classLoader = copyFrom.classLoader;
418         typePropertyName = copyFrom.typePropertyName;
419         useJavaBeanIntrospector = copyFrom.useJavaBeanIntrospector;
420         validateSchema = copyFrom.validateSchema;
421         beanStore = copyFrom.beanStore;
422         activeView = copyFrom.activeView;
423         disableDefaultViewInclusion = ! copyFrom.defaultViewInclusion;
424      }
425
426      /**
427       * Copy constructor.
428       *
429       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
430       */
431      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
432         super(copyFrom);
433         beanClassVisibility = copyFrom.beanClassVisibility;
434         beanConstructorVisibility = copyFrom.beanConstructorVisibility;
435         beanDictionary = copyOf(copyFrom.beanDictionary);
436         beanFieldVisibility = copyFrom.beanFieldVisibility;
437         beanMapPutReturnsOldValue = copyFrom.beanMapPutReturnsOldValue;
438         beanMethodVisibility = copyFrom.beanMethodVisibility;
439         beansRequireDefaultConstructor = copyFrom.beansRequireDefaultConstructor;
440         beansRequireSerializable = copyFrom.beansRequireSerializable;
441         beansRequireSettersForGetters = copyFrom.beansRequireSettersForGetters;
442         disableBeansRequireSomeProperties = copyFrom.disableBeansRequireSomeProperties;
443         disableIgnoreMissingSetters = copyFrom.disableIgnoreMissingSetters;
444         disableIgnoreTransientFields = copyFrom.disableIgnoreTransientFields;
445         disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties = copyFrom.disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties;
446         disableInterfaceProxies = copyFrom.disableInterfaceProxies;
447         findFluentSetters = copyFrom.findFluentSetters;
448         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters = copyFrom.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters;
449         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters = copyFrom.ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters;
450         ignoreUnknownBeanProperties = copyFrom.ignoreUnknownBeanProperties;
451         ignoreUnknownEnumValues = copyFrom.ignoreUnknownEnumValues;
452         locale = copyFrom.locale;
453         mediaType = copyFrom.mediaType;
454         notBeanClasses = new TreeSet<>(copyFrom.notBeanClasses);
455         notBeanPackages = toSortedSet(copyFrom.notBeanPackages);
456         propertyNamer = copyFrom.propertyNamer;
457         unsortedProperties = copyFrom.unsortedProperties;
458         swaps = copyOf(copyFrom.swaps);
459         timeZone = copyFrom.timeZone;
460         durationFormat = copyFrom.durationFormat;
461         periodFormat = copyFrom.periodFormat;
462         calendarFormat = copyFrom.calendarFormat;
463         dateFormat = copyFrom.dateFormat;
464         temporalFormat = copyFrom.temporalFormat;
465         timeZoneFormat = copyFrom.timeZoneFormat;
466         localeFormat = copyFrom.localeFormat;
467         binaryFormat = copyFrom.binaryFormat;
468         enumFormat = copyFrom.enumFormat;
469         uuidFormat = copyFrom.uuidFormat;
470         bitSetFormat = copyFrom.bitSetFormat;
471         bigNumberFormat = copyFrom.bigNumberFormat;
472         booleanFormat = copyFrom.booleanFormat;
473         floatFormat = copyFrom.floatFormat;
474         currencyFormat = copyFrom.currencyFormat;
475         classFormat = copyFrom.classFormat;
476         classLoader = copyFrom.classLoader;
477         typePropertyName = copyFrom.typePropertyName;
478         useJavaBeanIntrospector = copyFrom.useJavaBeanIntrospector;
479         validateSchema = copyFrom.validateSchema;
480         beanStore = copyFrom.beanStore;
481         activeView = copyFrom.activeView;
482         disableDefaultViewInclusion = copyFrom.disableDefaultViewInclusion;
483      }
484
485      /**
486       * Minimum bean class visibility.
487       *
488       * <p>
489       * Classes are not considered beans unless they meet the minimum visibility requirements.
490       * For example, if the visibility is <jsf>PUBLIC</jsf> and the bean class is <jk>protected</jk>, then the class
491       * will not be interpreted as a bean class and be serialized as a string.
492       * Use this setting to reduce the visibility requirement.
493       *
494       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
495       * <p class='bjava'>
496       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected class and one field.</jc>
497       *    <jk>protected class</jk> MyBean {
498       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
499       *    }
500       *
501       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that's capable of serializing the class.</jc>
502       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
503       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
504       *       .beanClassVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
505       *       .build();
506       *
507       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo","bar"}</jc>
508       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
509       * </p>
510       *
511       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
512       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Bean @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a non-public bean class to override this setting.
513       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean class to ignore it as a bean.
514       * </ul>
515       *
516       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
517       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanClassVisibility()}
518       * </ul>
519       *
520       * @param value
521       *    The new value for this setting.
522       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}.
523       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
524       * @return This object.
525       */
526      public Builder beanClassVisibility(Visibility value) {
527         beanClassVisibility = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
528         return this;
529      }
530
531      /**
532       * Minimum bean constructor visibility.
533       *
534       * <p>
535       * Only look for constructors with the specified minimum visibility.
536       *
537       * <p>
538       * This setting affects the logic for finding no-arg constructors for bean.  Normally, only <jk>public</jk> no-arg
539       * constructors are used.  Use this setting if you want to reduce the visibility requirement.
540       *
541       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
542       * <p class='bjava'>
543       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected constructor and one field.</jc>
544       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
545       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>;
546       *
547       *       <jk>protected</jk> MyBean() {}
548       *    }
549       *
550       *    <jc>// Create a parser capable of calling the protected constructor.</jc>
551       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = ReaderParser
552       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
553       *       .beanConstructorVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
554       *       .build();
555       *
556       *    <jc>// Use it.</jc>
557       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
558       * </p>
559       *
560       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
561       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotation can also be used to expose a non-public constructor.
562       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean constructor to ignore it.
563       * </ul>
564       *
565       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
566       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanConstructorVisibility()}
567       * </ul>
568       *
569       * @param value
570       *    The new value for this setting.
571       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}.
572       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
573       * @return This object.
574       */
575      public Builder beanConstructorVisibility(Visibility value) {
576         beanConstructorVisibility = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
577         return this;
578      }
579
580      /**
581       * Returns the bean dictionary list.
582       *
583       * <p>
584       * Gives access to the inner list if you need to make more than simple additions via {@link #beanDictionary(Class...)}.
585       *
586       * @return The bean dictionary list.
587       * @see #beanDictionary(Class...)
588       */
589      public List<ClassInfo> beanDictionary() {
590         return beanDictionary;
591      }
592
593      /**
594       * Convenience method for {@link #beanDictionary(ClassInfo...)} that accepts {@link Class} objects.
595       *
596       * @param values
597       *    The values to add to this setting.
598       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
599       * @return This object.
600       * @see #beanDictionary(ClassInfo...)
601       */
602      public Builder beanDictionary(Class<?>...values) {
603         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
604         return beanDictionary(Stream.of(values).map(x -> info(x)).toArray(ClassInfo[]::new));
605      }
606
607      /**
608       * Bean dictionary.
609       *
610       * <p>
611       * The list of classes that make up the bean dictionary in this bean context.
612       *
613       * <p>
614       * Values are prepended to the list so that later calls can override classes of earlier calls.
615       *
616       * <p>
617       * A dictionary is a name/class mapping used to find class types during parsing when they cannot be inferred
618       * through reflection.  The names are defined through the {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)} annotation defined
619       * on the bean class.  For example, if a class <c>Foo</c> has a type-name of <js>"myfoo"</js>, then it would end up
620       * serialized as <js>"{_type:'myfoo',...}"</js> in JSON
621       * or <js>"&lt;myfoo&gt;...&lt;/myfoo&gt;"</js> in XML.
622       *
623       * <p>
624       * This setting tells the parsers which classes to look for when resolving <js>"_type"</js> attributes.
625       *
626       * <p>
627       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
628       * <ul>
629       *    <li>Any bean class that specifies a value for {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)}.
630       *    <li>Any subclass of {@link BeanDictionaryList} containing a collection of bean classes with type name annotations.
631       *    <li>Any subclass of {@link BeanDictionaryMap} containing a mapping of type names to classes without type name annotations.
632       *    <li>Any array or collection of the objects above.
633       * </ul>
634       *
635       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
636       * <p class='bjava'>
637       *    <jc>// POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations.</jc>
638       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"foo"</js>)
639       *    <jk>public class</jk> Foo {...}
640       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"bar"</js>)
641       *    <jk>public class</jk> Bar {...}
642       *
643       *    <jc>// Create a parser and tell it which classes to try to resolve.</jc>
644       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
645       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
646       *       .dictionary(Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
647       *       .addBeanTypes()
648       *       .build();
649       *
650       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an indeterminate type.</jc>
651       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
652       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;
653       *    }
654       *
655       *    <jc>// Parse bean.</jc>
656       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{mySimpleField:{_type:'foo',...}}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
657       * </p>
658       *
659       * <p>
660       * Another option is to use the {@link Marshalled#dictionary()} annotation on the POJO class itself:
661       *
662       * <p class='bjava'>
663       *    <jc>// Instead of by parser, define a bean dictionary on a class through an annotation.</jc>
664       *    <jc>// This applies to all properties on this class and all subclasses.</jc>
665       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(dictionary={Foo.<jk>class</jk>,Bar.<jk>class</jk>})
666       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
667       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;  <jc>// May contain Foo or Bar object.</jc>
668       *       <jk>public</jk> Map&lt;String,Object&gt; <jf>myMapField</jf>;  <jc>// May contain Foo or Bar objects.</jc>
669       *    }
670       * </p>
671       *
672       * <p>
673       *    A typical usage is to allow for HTML documents to be parsed back into HTML beans:
674       * <p class='bjava'>
675       *    <jc>// Use the predefined HTML5 bean dictionary which is a BeanDictionaryList.</jc>
676       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = HtmlParser
677       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
678       *       .dictionary(HtmlBeanDictionary.<jk>class</jk>)
679       *       .build();
680       *
681       *    <jc>// Parse an HTML body into HTML beans.</jc>
682       *    Body <jv>body</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"&lt;body&gt;&lt;ul&gt;&lt;li&gt;foo&lt;/li&gt;&lt;li&gt;bar&lt;/li&gt;&lt;/ul&gt;"</js>, Body.<jk>class</jk>);
683       * </p>
684       *
685       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
686       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#dictionary()}
687       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledProp#dictionary()}
688       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#dictionary()}
689       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#dictionary_replace()}
690       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingContext.Builder#beanDictionary(ClassInfo...)}
691       * </ul>
692       *
693       * @param values
694       *    The values to add to this setting.
695       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
696       * @return This object.
697       */
698      public Builder beanDictionary(ClassInfo...values) {
699         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
700         return beanDictionary(l(values));
701      }
702
703      /**
704       * Same as {@link #beanDictionary(ClassInfo...)} but allows you to pass in a collection of class info objects.
705       *
706       * @param values
707       *    The values to add to this setting.
708       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> values.
709       * @return This object.
710       * @see #beanDictionary(ClassInfo...)
711       */
712      public Builder beanDictionary(Collection<ClassInfo> values) {
713         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
714         beanDictionary().addAll(0, values);
715         return this;
716      }
717
718      /**
719       * Minimum bean field visibility.
720       *
721       * <p>
722       * Only look for bean fields with the specified minimum visibility.
723       *
724       * <p>
725       * This affects which fields on a bean class are considered bean properties.  Normally only <jk>public</jk> fields are considered.
726       * Use this setting if you want to reduce the visibility requirement.
727       *
728       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
729       * <p class='bjava'>
730       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected field.</jc>
731       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
732       *       <jk>protected</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
733       *    }
734       *
735       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that recognizes the protected field.</jc>
736       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
737       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
738       *       .beanFieldVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
739       *       .build();
740       *
741       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"bar"}</jc>
742       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
743       * </p>
744       *
745       * <p>
746       * Bean fields can be ignored as properties entirely by setting the value to {@link Visibility#NONE}
747       *
748       * <p class='bjava'>
749       *    <jc>// Disable using fields as properties entirely.</jc>
750       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
751       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
752       *       .beanFieldVisibility(<jsf>NONE</jsf>)
753       *       .build();
754       * </p>
755       *
756       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
757       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used to expose a non-public field.
758       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean field to ignore it as a bean property.
759       * </ul>
760       *
761       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
762       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanFieldVisibility()}
763       * </ul>
764       *
765       * @param value
766       *    The new value for this setting.
767       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}.
768       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
769       * @return This object.
770       */
771      public Builder beanFieldVisibility(Visibility value) {
772         beanFieldVisibility = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
773         return this;
774      }
775
776      /**
777       * Bean interceptor.
778       *
779       * <p>
780       * Bean interceptors can be used to intercept calls to getters and setters and alter their values in transit.
781       *
782       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
783       * <p class='bjava'>
784       *    <jc>// Interceptor that strips out sensitive information.</jc>
785       *    <jk>public class</jk> AddressInterceptor <jk>extends</jk> BeanInterceptor&lt;Address&gt; {
786       *
787       *       <jk>public</jk> Object readProperty(Address <jv>bean</jv>, String <jv>name</jv>, Object <jv>value</jv>) {
788       *          <jk>if</jk> (<js>"taxInfo"</js>.equals(<jv>name</jv>))
789       *             <jk>return</jk> <js>"redacted"</js>;
790       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>value</jv>;
791       *       }
792       *
793       *       <jk>public</jk> Object writeProperty(Address <jv>bean</jv>, String <jv>name</jv>, Object <jv>value</jv>) {
794       *          <jk>if</jk> (<js>"taxInfo"</js>.equals(<jv>name</jv>) &amp;&amp; <js>"redacted"</js>.equals(<jv>value</jv>))
795       *             <jk>return</jk> TaxInfoUtils.<jsm>lookup</jsm>(<jv>bean</jv>.getStreet(), <jv>bean</jv>.getCity(), <jv>bean</jv>.getState());
796       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>value</jv>;
797       *       }
798       *    }
799       *
800       *    <jc>// Our bean class.</jc>
801       *    <jk>public class</jk> Address {
802       *       <jk>public</jk> String getTaxInfo() {...}
803       *       <jk>public void</jk> setTaxInfo(String <jv>value</jv>) {...}
804       *    }
805       *
806       *    <jc>// Register filter on serializer or parser.</jc>
807       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
808       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
809       *       .beanInterceptor(Address.<jk>class</jk>, AddressInterceptor.<jk>class</jk>)
810       *       .build();
811       *
812       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"taxInfo":"redacted"}</jc>
813       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> Address());
814       * </p>
815       *
816       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
817       *    <li class='jc'>{@link BeanInterceptor}
818       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#interceptor() @Marshalled(interceptor)}
819       * </ul>
820       *
821       * @param on The bean that the filter applies to.
822       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
823       * @param value
824       *    The new value for this setting.
825       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
826       * @return This object.
827       */
828      public Builder beanInterceptor(Class<?> on, Class<? extends BeanInterceptor<?>> value) {
829         assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
830         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
831         return annotations(MarshalledApplyAnnotation.create(on).value(MarshalledAnnotation.create().interceptor(value).build()).build());
832      }
833
834      /**
835       * BeanMap.put() returns old property value.
836       *
837       * <p>
838       * When enabled, then the {@link BeanMap#put(String,Object) BeanMap.put()} method will return old property
839       * values.  Otherwise, it returns <jk>null</jk>.
840       *
841       * <p>
842       * Disabled by default because it introduces a slight performance penalty during serialization.
843       *
844       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
845       * <p class='bjava'>
846       *    <jc>// Create a context that creates BeanMaps with normal put() behavior.</jc>
847       *    MarshallingContext <jv>context</jv> = MarshallingContext
848       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
849       *       .beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()
850       *       .build();
851       *
852       *    BeanMap&lt;MyBean&gt; <jv>myBeanMap</jv> = <jv>context</jv>.createSession().toBeanMap(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
853       *    <jv>myBeanMap</jv>.put(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>);
854       *    Object <jv>oldValue</jv> = <jv>myBeanMap</jv>.put(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"baz"</js>);  <jc>// oldValue == "bar"</jc>
855       * </p>
856       *
857       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
858       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()}
859       * </ul>
860       *
861       * @return This object.
862       */
863      public Builder beanMapPutReturnsOldValue() {
864         return beanMapPutReturnsOldValue(true);
865      }
866
867      /**
868       * Same as {@link #beanMapPutReturnsOldValue()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
869       *
870       * @param value The value for this setting.
871       * @return This object.
872       */
873      public Builder beanMapPutReturnsOldValue(boolean value) {
874         beanMapPutReturnsOldValue = value;
875         return this;
876      }
877
878      /**
879       * Minimum bean method visibility.
880       *
881       * <p>
882       * Only look for bean methods with the specified minimum visibility.
883       *
884       * <p>
885       * This affects which methods are detected as getters and setters on a bean class. Normally only <jk>public</jk> getters and setters are considered.
886       * Use this setting if you want to reduce the visibility requirement.
887       *
888       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
889       * <p class='bjava'>
890       *    <jc>// A bean with a protected getter.</jc>
891       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
892       *       <jk>public</jk> String getFoo() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"foo"</js>; }
893       *       <jk>protected</jk> String getBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>; }
894       *    }
895       *
896       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that looks for protected getters and setters.</jc>
897       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
898       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
899       *       .beanMethodVisibility(<jsf>PROTECTED</jsf>)
900       *       .build();
901       *
902       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
903       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
904       * </p>
905       *
906       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
907       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used to expose a non-public method.
908       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a public bean getter/setter to ignore it as a bean property.
909       * </ul>
910       *
911       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
912       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beanMethodVisibility()}
913       * </ul>
914       *
915       * @param value
916       *    The new value for this setting.
917       *    <br>The default is {@link Visibility#PUBLIC}
918       * @return This object.
919       */
920      public Builder beanMethodVisibility(Visibility value) {
921         beanMethodVisibility = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
922         return this;
923      }
924
925      /**
926       * Bean property includes.
927       *
928       * <p>
929       * Specifies the set and order of names of properties associated with the bean class.
930       *
931       * <p>
932       * For example, <c>beanProperties(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)</c> means only serialize the <c>foo</c> and
933       * <c>bar</c> properties on the specified bean.  Likewise, parsing will ignore any bean properties not specified
934       * and either throw an exception or silently ignore them depending on whether {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
935       * has been called.
936       *
937       * <p>
938       * This value is entirely optional if you simply want to expose all the getters and public fields on
939       * a class as bean properties.  However, it's useful if you want certain getters to be ignored or you want the properties to be
940       * serialized in a particular order.  Note that on IBM JREs, the property order is the same as the order in the source code,
941       * whereas on Oracle JREs, the order is entirely random.
942       *
943       * <p>
944       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
945       *
946       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
947       * <p class='bjava'>
948       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
949       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
950       *       <jk>public</jk> String
951       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
952       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
953       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
954       *    }
955       *
956       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that includes only the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
957       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
958       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
959       *       .beanProperties(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)
960       *       .build();
961       *
962       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
963       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
964       * </p>
965       *
966       * <p>
967       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
968       * <p class='bjava'>
969       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).properties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
970       * </p>
971       *
972       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
973       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#properties()}/{@link BeanType#p()} - On an annotation on the bean class itself.
974       * </ul>
975       *
976       * @param beanClass The bean class.
977       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
978       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
979       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
980       * @return This object.
981       */
982      public Builder beanProperties(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
983         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClass, beanClass);
984         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
985         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClass).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().p(properties).build()).build());
986      }
987
988      /**
989       * Bean property includes.
990       *
991       * <p>
992       * Specifies the set and order of names of properties associated with bean classes.
993       *
994       * <p>
995       * For example, <c>beanProperties(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>))</c> means only serialize the <c>foo</c> and
996       * <c>bar</c> properties on the specified bean.  Likewise, parsing will ignore any bean properties not specified
997       * and either throw an exception or silently ignore them depending on whether {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
998       * has been called.
999       *
1000       * <p>
1001       * This value is entirely optional if you simply want to expose all the getters and public fields on
1002       * a class as bean properties.  However, it's useful if you want certain getters to be ignored or you want the properties to be
1003       * serialized in a particular order.  Note that on IBM JREs, the property order is the same as the order in the source code,
1004       * whereas on Oracle JREs, the order is entirely random.
1005       *
1006       * <p>
1007       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
1008       *
1009       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1010       * <p class='bjava'>
1011       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1012       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1013       *       <jk>public</jk> String
1014       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
1015       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
1016       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
1017       *    }
1018       *
1019       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that includes only the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1020       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1021       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1022       *       .beanProperties(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>))
1023       *       .build();
1024       *
1025       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
1026       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1027       * </p>
1028       *
1029       * <p>
1030       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
1031       * <p class='bjava'>
1032       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).properties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
1033       * </p>
1034       *
1035       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1036       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#properties()} / {@link BeanType#p()}- On an annotation on the bean class itself.
1037       * </ul>
1038       *
1039       * @param values
1040       *    The values to add to this builder.
1041       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1042       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
1043       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1044       * @return This object.
1045       */
1046      public Builder beanProperties(Map<String,Object> values) {
1047         assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
1048         values.forEach((k, v) -> annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(k).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().p(s(v)).build()).build()));
1049         return this;
1050      }
1051
1052      /**
1053       * Bean property includes.
1054       *
1055       * <p>
1056       * Specifies the set and order of names of properties associated with the bean class.
1057       *
1058       * <p>
1059       * For example, <c>beanProperties(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)</c> means only serialize the <c>foo</c> and
1060       * <c>bar</c> properties on the specified bean.  Likewise, parsing will ignore any bean properties not specified
1061       * and either throw an exception or silently ignore them depending on whether {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
1062       * has been called.
1063       *
1064       * <p>
1065       * This value is entirely optional if you simply want to expose all the getters and public fields on
1066       * a class as bean properties.  However, it's useful if you want certain getters to be ignored or you want the properties to be
1067       * serialized in a particular order.  Note that on IBM JREs, the property order is the same as the order in the source code,
1068       * whereas on Oracle JREs, the order is entirely random.
1069       *
1070       * <p>
1071       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
1072       *
1073       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1074       * <p class='bjava'>
1075       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1076       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1077       *       <jk>public</jk> String
1078       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
1079       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
1080       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
1081       *    }
1082       *
1083       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that includes only the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1084       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1085       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1086       *       .beanProperties(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"foo,bar"</js>)
1087       *       .build();
1088       *
1089       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo","bar":"bar"}</jc>
1090       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1091       * </p>
1092       *
1093       * <p>
1094       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1095       * <p class='bjava'>
1096       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).properties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1097       * </p>
1098       *
1099       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1100       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#properties()} / {@link BeanType#p()} - On an annotation on the bean class itself.
1101       * </ul>
1102       *
1103       * @param beanClassName
1104       *    The bean class name.
1105       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1106       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1107       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1108       * @return This object.
1109       */
1110      public Builder beanProperties(String beanClassName, String properties) {
1111         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName);
1112         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1113         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClassName).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().p(properties).build()).build());
1114      }
1115
1116      /**
1117       * Bean property excludes.
1118       *
1119       * <p>
1120       * Specifies to exclude the specified list of properties for the specified bean class.
1121       *
1122       * <p>
1123       * Same as {@link #beanProperties(Class, String)} except you specify a list of bean property names that you want to exclude from
1124       * serialization.
1125       *
1126       * <p>
1127       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
1128       *
1129       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1130       * <p class='bjava'>
1131       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1132       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1133       *       <jk>public</jk> String
1134       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
1135       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
1136       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
1137       *    }
1138       *
1139       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the "bar" and "baz" properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1140       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1141       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1142       *       .beanPropertiesExcludes(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1143       *       .build();
1144       *
1145       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
1146       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1147       * </p>
1148       *
1149       * <p>
1150       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1151       * <p class='bjava'>
1152       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).excludeProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1153       * </p>
1154       *
1155       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1156       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#excludeProperties()} / {@link BeanType#xp()}
1157       * </ul>
1158       *
1159       * @param beanClass The bean class.
1160       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1161       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1162       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1163       * @return This object.
1164       */
1165      public Builder beanPropertiesExcludes(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
1166         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClass, beanClass);
1167         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1168         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClass).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().xp(properties).build()).build());
1169      }
1170
1171      /**
1172       * Bean property excludes.
1173       *
1174       * <p>
1175       * Specifies to exclude the specified list of properties for the specified bean classes.
1176       *
1177       * <p>
1178       * Same as {@link #beanProperties(Map)} except you specify a list of bean property names that you want to exclude from
1179       * serialization.
1180       *
1181       * <p>
1182       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
1183       *
1184       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1185       * <p class='bjava'>
1186       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1187       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1188       *       <jk>public</jk> String
1189       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
1190       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
1191       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
1192       *    }
1193       *
1194       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the "bar" and "baz" properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1195       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1196       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1197       *       .beanPropertiesExcludes(AMap.of(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1198       *       .build();
1199       *
1200       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
1201       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1202       * </p>
1203       *
1204       * <p>
1205       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
1206       * <p class='bjava'>
1207       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).excludeProperties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
1208       * </p>
1209       *
1210       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1211       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#excludeProperties()} / {@link BeanType#xp()}
1212       * </ul>
1213       *
1214       * @param values
1215       *    The values to add to this builder.
1216       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1217       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
1218       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1219       * @return This object.
1220       */
1221      public Builder beanPropertiesExcludes(Map<String,Object> values) {
1222         assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
1223         values.forEach((k, v) -> annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(k).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().xp(s(v)).build()).build()));
1224         return this;
1225      }
1226
1227      /**
1228       * Bean property excludes.
1229       *
1230       * <p>
1231       * Specifies to exclude the specified list of properties for the specified bean class.
1232       *
1233       * <p>
1234       * Same as {@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(String, String)} except you specify a list of bean property names that you want to exclude from
1235       * serialization.
1236       *
1237       * <p>
1238       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
1239       *
1240       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1241       * <p class='bjava'>
1242       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1243       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1244       *       <jk>public</jk> String
1245       *          <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>,
1246       *          <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>,
1247       *          <jf>baz</jf> = <js>"baz"</js>;
1248       *    }
1249       *
1250       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the "bar" and "baz" properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
1251       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1252       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1253       *       .beanPropertiesExcludes(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1254       *       .build();
1255       *
1256       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
1257       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1258       * </p>
1259       *
1260       * <p>
1261       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1262       * <p class='bjava'>
1263       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).excludeProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1264       * </p>
1265       *
1266       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1267       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#excludeProperties()} / {@link BeanType#xp()}
1268       * </ul>
1269       *
1270       * @param beanClassName
1271       *    The bean class name.
1272       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all bean classes.
1273       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1274       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1275       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1276       * @return This object.
1277       */
1278      public Builder beanPropertiesExcludes(String beanClassName, String properties) {
1279         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName);
1280         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1281         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClassName).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().xp(properties).build()).build());
1282      }
1283
1284      /**
1285       * Read-only bean properties.
1286       *
1287       * <p>
1288       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are read-only despite having valid getters.
1289       * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
1290       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1291       * for both serializers and parsers.
1292       *
1293       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1294       * <p class='bjava'>
1295       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1296       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1297       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1298       *    }
1299       *
1300       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with read-only property settings.</jc>
1301       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1302       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1303       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1304       *       .build();
1305       *
1306       *    <jc>// All 3 properties will be serialized.</jc>
1307       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1308       *
1309       *    <jc>// Create a parser with read-only property settings.</jc>
1310       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1311       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1312       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1313       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
1314       *       .build();
1315       *
1316       *    <jc>// Parser ignores bar and baz properties.</jc>
1317       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1318       * </p>
1319       *
1320       * <p>
1321       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1322       * <p class='bjava'>
1323       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).readOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1324       * </p>
1325       *
1326       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1327       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#readOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#ro()}
1328       * </ul>
1329       *
1330       * @param beanClass The bean class.
1331       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1332       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1333       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1334       * @return This object.
1335       */
1336      public Builder beanPropertiesReadOnly(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
1337         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClass, beanClass);
1338         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1339         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClass).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().ro(properties).build()).build());
1340      }
1341
1342      /**
1343       * Read-only bean properties.
1344       *
1345       * <p>
1346       * Specifies one or more properties on beans that are read-only despite having valid getters.
1347       * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
1348       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1349       * for both serializers and parsers.
1350       *
1351       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1352       * <p class='bjava'>
1353       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1354       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1355       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1356       *    }
1357       *
1358       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with read-only property settings.</jc>
1359       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1360       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1361       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1362       *       .build();
1363       *
1364       *    <jc>// All 3 properties will be serialized.</jc>
1365       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1366       *
1367       *    <jc>// Create a parser with read-only property settings.</jc>
1368       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1369       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1370       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1371       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
1372       *       .build();
1373       *
1374       *    <jc>// Parser ignores bar and baz properties.</jc>
1375       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1376       * </p>
1377       *
1378       * <p>
1379       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
1380       * <p class='bjava'>
1381       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).readOnlyProperties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
1382       * </p>
1383       *
1384       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1385       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#readOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#ro()}
1386       * </ul>
1387       *
1388       * @param values
1389       *    The values to add to this builder.
1390       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1391       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
1392       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1393       * @return This object.
1394       */
1395      public Builder beanPropertiesReadOnly(Map<String,Object> values) {
1396         assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
1397         values.forEach((k, v) -> annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(k).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().ro(s(v)).build()).build()));
1398         return this;
1399      }
1400
1401      /**
1402       * Read-only bean properties.
1403       *
1404       * <p>
1405       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are read-only despite having valid getters.
1406       * Serializers will serialize such properties as usual, but parsers will silently ignore them.
1407       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1408       * for both serializers and parsers.
1409       *
1410       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1411       * <p class='bjava'>
1412       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1413       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1414       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1415       *    }
1416       *
1417       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with read-only property settings.</jc>
1418       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1419       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1420       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1421       *       .build();
1422       *
1423       *    <jc>// All 3 properties will be serialized.</jc>
1424       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1425       *
1426       *    <jc>// Create a parser with read-only property settings.</jc>
1427       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1428       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1429       *       .beanPropertiesReadOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1430       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
1431       *       .build();
1432       *
1433       *    <jc>// Parser ignores bar and baz properties.</jc>
1434       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1435       * </p>
1436       *
1437       * <p>
1438       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1439       * <p class='bjava'>
1440       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).readOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1441       * </p>
1442       *
1443       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1444       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#readOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#ro()}
1445       * </ul>
1446       *
1447       * @param beanClassName
1448       *    The bean class name.
1449       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all bean classes.
1450       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1451       * @return This object.
1452       */
1453      public Builder beanPropertiesReadOnly(String beanClassName, String properties) {
1454         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName);
1455         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1456         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClassName).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().ro(properties).build()).build());
1457      }
1458
1459      /**
1460       * Write-only bean properties.
1461       *
1462       * <p>
1463       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
1464       * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
1465       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1466       * for both serializers and parsers.
1467       *
1468       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1469       * <p class='bjava'>
1470       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1471       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1472       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1473       *    }
1474       *
1475       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with write-only property settings.</jc>
1476       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1477       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1478       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1479       *       .build();
1480       *
1481       *    <jc>// Only foo will be serialized.</jc>
1482       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1483       *
1484       *    <jc>// Create a parser with write-only property settings.</jc>
1485       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1486       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1487       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1488       *       .build();
1489       *
1490       *    <jc>// Parser parses all 3 properties.</jc>
1491       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1492       * </p>
1493       *
1494       * <p>
1495       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1496       * <p class='bjava'>
1497       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClass</jv>).writeOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1498       * </p>
1499       *
1500       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1501       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#writeOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#wo()}
1502       * </ul>
1503       *
1504       * @param beanClass The bean class.
1505       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1506       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1507       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1508       * @return This object.
1509       */
1510      public Builder beanPropertiesWriteOnly(Class<?> beanClass, String properties) {
1511         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClass, beanClass);
1512         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1513         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClass).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().wo(properties).build()).build());
1514      }
1515
1516      /**
1517       * Write-only bean properties.
1518       *
1519       * <p>
1520       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
1521       * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
1522       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1523       * for both serializers and parsers.
1524       *
1525       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1526       * <p class='bjava'>
1527       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1528       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1529       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1530       *    }
1531       *
1532       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with write-only property settings.</jc>
1533       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1534       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1535       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1536       *       .build();
1537       *
1538       *    <jc>// Only foo will be serialized.</jc>
1539       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1540       *
1541       *    <jc>// Create a parser with write-only property settings.</jc>
1542       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1543       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1544       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>))
1545       *       .build();
1546       *
1547       *    <jc>// Parser parses all 3 properties.</jc>
1548       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1549       * </p>
1550       *
1551       * <p>
1552       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code for each entry:
1553       * <p class='bjava'>
1554       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>key</jv>).writeOnlyProperties(<jv>value</jv>.toString()).build());
1555       * </p>
1556       *
1557       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1558       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#writeOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#wo()}
1559       * </ul>
1560       *
1561       * @param values
1562       *    The values to add to this builder.
1563       *    <br>Keys are bean class names which can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all beans.
1564       *    <br>Values are comma-delimited lists of property names.  Non-String objects are first converted to Strings.
1565       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1566       * @return This object.
1567       */
1568      public Builder beanPropertiesWriteOnly(Map<String,Object> values) {
1569         assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
1570         values.forEach((k, v) -> annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(k).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().wo(s(v)).build()).build()));
1571         return this;
1572      }
1573
1574      /**
1575       * Write-only bean properties.
1576       *
1577       * <p>
