1578       * Specifies one or more properties on a bean that are write-only despite having valid setters.
1579       * Parsers will parse such properties as usual, but serializers will silently ignore them.
1580       * Note that this is different from the {@link #beanProperties(Class,String) beanProperties}/{@link #beanPropertiesExcludes(Class,String) beanPropertiesExcludes} settings which include or exclude properties
1581       * for both serializers and parsers.
1582       *
1583       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1584       * <p class='bjava'>
1585       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
1586       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1587       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>, <jf>bar</jf>, <jf>baz</jf>;
1588       *    }
1589       *
1590       *    <jc>// Create a serializer with write-only property settings.</jc>
1591       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1592       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1593       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1594       *       .build();
1595       *
1596       *    <jc>// Only foo will be serialized.</jc>
1597       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1598       *
1599       *    <jc>// Create a parser with write-only property settings.</jc>
1600       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1601       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1602       *       .beanPropertiesWriteOnly(<js>"MyBean"</js>, <js>"bar,baz"</js>)
1603       *       .build();
1604       *
1605       *    <jc>// Parser parses all 3 properties.</jc>
1606       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar',baz:'baz'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1607       * </p>
1608       *
1609       * <p>
1610       * This method is functionally equivalent to the following code:
1611       * <p class='bjava'>
1612       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>beanClassName</jv>).writeOnlyProperties(<jv>properties</jv>).build());
1613       * </p>
1614       *
1615       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1616       *    <li class='jma'>{@link BeanType#writeOnlyProperties()} / {@link BeanType#wo()}
1617       * </ul>
1618       *
1619       * @param beanClassName
1620       *    The bean class name.
1621       *    <br>Can be a simple name, fully-qualified name, or <js>"*"</js> for all bean classes.
1622       * @param properties Comma-delimited list of property names.
1623       * @return This object.
1624       */
1625      public Builder beanPropertiesWriteOnly(String beanClassName, String properties) {
1626         assertArgNotNull(ARG_beanClassName, beanClassName);
1627         assertArgNotNull(ARG_properties, properties);
1628         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(beanClassName).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().wo(properties).build()).build());
1629      }
1630
1631      /**
1632       * Beans require no-arg constructors.
1633       *
1634       * <p>
1635       * When enabled, a Java class must implement a default no-arg constructor to be considered a bean.
1636       * Otherwise, the bean will be serialized as a string using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
1637       *
1638       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1639       * <p class='bjava'>
1640       *    <jc>// A bean without a no-arg constructor.</jc>
1641       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1642       *
1643       *       <jc>// A property method.</jc>
1644       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
1645       *
1646       *       <jc>// A no-arg constructor</jc>
1647       *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean(String <jv>foo</jv>) {
1648       *          <jk>this</jk>.<jf>foo</jf> = <jv>foo</jv>;
1649       *       }
1650       *
1651       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1652       *       <jk>public</jk> String toString() {
1653       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>;
1654       *       }
1655       *    }
1656       *
1657       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores beans without default constructors.</jc>
1658       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1659       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1660       *       .beansRequireDefaultConstructor()
1661       *       .build();
1662       *
1663       *    <jc>// Produces:  "bar"</jc>
1664       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1665       * </p>
1666       *
1667       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1668       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Bean @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a bean class to override this setting.
1669       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a class to ignore it as a bean.
1670       * </ul>
1671       *
1672       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1673       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beansRequireDefaultConstructor()}
1674       * </ul>
1675       *
1676       * @return This object.
1677       */
1678      public Builder beansRequireDefaultConstructor() {
1679         return beansRequireDefaultConstructor(true);
1680      }
1681
1682      /**
1683       * Same as {@link #beansRequireDefaultConstructor()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
1684       *
1685       * @param value The value for this setting.
1686       * @return This object.
1687       */
1688      public Builder beansRequireDefaultConstructor(boolean value) {
1689         beansRequireDefaultConstructor = value;
1690         return this;
1691      }
1692
1693      /**
1694       * Beans require Serializable interface.
1695       *
1696       * <p>
1697       * When enabled, a Java class must implement the {@link Serializable} interface to be considered a bean.
1698       * Otherwise, the bean will be serialized as a string using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
1699       *
1700       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1701       * <p class='bjava'>
1702       *    <jc>// A bean without a Serializable interface.</jc>
1703       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1704       *
1705       *       <jc>// A property method.</jc>
1706       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
1707       *
1708       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1709       *       <jk>public</jk> String toString() {
1710       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>;
1711       *       }
1712       *    }
1713       *
1714       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores beans not implementing Serializable.</jc>
1715       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1716       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1717       *       .beansRequireSerializable()
1718       *       .build();
1719       *
1720       *    <jc>// Produces:  "bar"</jc>
1721       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1722       * </p>
1723       *
1724       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1725       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Bean @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a bean class to override this setting.
1726       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on a class to ignore it as a bean.
1727       * </ul>
1728       *
1729       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1730       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#beansRequireSerializable()}
1731       * </ul>
1732       *
1733       * @return This object.
1734       */
1735      public Builder beansRequireSerializable() {
1736         return beansRequireSerializable(true);
1737      }
1738
1739      /**
1740       * Same as {@link #beansRequireSerializable()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
1741       *
1742       * @param value The value for this setting.
1743       * @return This object.
1744       */
1745      public Builder beansRequireSerializable(boolean value) {
1746         beansRequireSerializable = value;
1747         return this;
1748      }
1749
1750      /**
1751       * Beans require setters for getters.
1752       *
1753       * <p>
1754       * When enabled, ignore read-only properties (properties with getters but not setters).
1755       *
1756       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1757       * <p class='bjava'>
1758       *    <jc>// A bean without a Serializable interface.</jc>
1759       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1760       *
1761       *       <jc>// A read/write property.</jc>
1762       *       <jk>public</jk> String getFoo() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"foo"</js>; }
1763       *       <jk>public void</jk> setFoo(String <jv>foo</jv>) { ... }
1764       *
1765       *       <jc>// A read-only property.</jc>
1766       *       <jk>public</jk> String getBar() { <jk>return</jk> <js>"bar"</js>; }
1767       *    }
1768       *
1769       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores bean properties without setters.</jc>
1770       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1771       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1772       *       .beansRequireSettersForGetters()
1773       *       .build();
1774       *
1775       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
1776       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1777       * </p>
1778       *
1779       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1780       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can be used on the getter to override this setting.
1781       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on getters to ignore them as bean properties.
1782       * </ul>
1783       *
1784       * @return This object.
1785       */
1786      public Builder beansRequireSettersForGetters() {
1787         return beansRequireSettersForGetters(true);
1788      }
1789
1790      /**
1791       * Same as {@link #beansRequireSettersForGetters()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
1792       *
1793       * @param value The value for this setting.
1794       * @return This object.
1795       */
1796      public Builder beansRequireSettersForGetters(boolean value) {
1797         beansRequireSettersForGetters = value;
1798         return this;
1799      }
1800
1801      /**
1802       * Sets the bean store used for factory-based instantiation.
1803       *
1804       * <p>
1805       * The bean store is used to resolve {@link BeanFactory} instances registered via
1806       * <ja>@BeanType</ja><c>(factory=X.class)</c> and <ja>@BeanProp</ja><c>(factory=X.class)</c> annotations.
1807       * When a factory class is encountered, the framework first looks it up in the bean store
1808       * before attempting direct instantiation.
1809       *
1810       * <p>
1811       * Typically set to a {@code SpringBeanStore} wrapping the application's
1812       * {@code ApplicationContext} so that Spring-managed factories are resolved automatically.
1813       *
1814       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1815       * <p class='bjava'>
1816       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRestServlet <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
1817       *       <ja>@Autowired</ja> ApplicationContext <jv>ctx</jv>;
1818       *
1819       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1820       *       <jk>protected</jk> MarshallingContext createBeanContext(MarshallingContext.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv>) {
1821       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>builder</jv>.beanStore(<jk>new</jk> SpringBeanStore(<jv>ctx</jv>)).build();
1822       *       }
1823       *    }
1824       * </p>
1825       *
1826       * @param value The bean store, or <jk>null</jk> to use direct instantiation only.
1827       * @return This object.
1828       */
1829      public Builder beanStore(BeanStore value) {
1830         beanStore = value;
1831         return this;
1832      }
1833
1834      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
1835      public MarshallingContext build() {
1836         return cache(CACHE).build(MarshallingContext.class);
1837      }
1838
1839      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
1840      public Builder copy() {
1841         return new Builder(this);
1842      }
1843
1844      /**
1845       * Bean dictionary.
1846       *
1847       * <p>
1848       * This is identical to {@link #beanDictionary(Class...)}, but specifies a dictionary within the context of
1849       * a single class as opposed to globally.
1850       *
1851       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1852       * <p class='bjava'>
1853       *    <jc>// POJOs with @Marshalled(name) annotations.</jc>
1854       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"foo"</js>)
1855       *    <jk>public class</jk> Foo {...}
1856       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"bar"</js>)
1857       *    <jk>public class</jk> Bar {...}
1858       *
1859       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an indeterminate type.</jc>
1860       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1861       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>mySimpleField</jf>;
1862       *    }
1863       *
1864       *    <jc>// Create a parser and tell it which classes to try to resolve.</jc>
1865       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1866       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1867       *       .dictionaryOn(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, Foo.<jk>class</jk>, Bar.<jk>class</jk>)
1868       *       .build();
1869       *
1870       *    <jc>// Parse bean.</jc>
1871       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{mySimpleField:{_type:'foo',...}}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1872       * </p>
1873       *
1874       * <p>
1875       * This is functionally equivalent to the {@link Marshalled#dictionary()} annotation.
1876       *
1877       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1878       *    <li class='jma'>{@link Marshalled#dictionary()}
1879       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #beanDictionary(Class...)}
1880       * </ul>
1881       *
1882       * @param on The class that the dictionary values apply to.
1883       * @param values
1884       *    The new values for this setting.
1885       * @return This object.
1886       */
1887      public Builder dictionaryOn(Class<?> on, Class<?>...values) {
1888         assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
1889         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
1890         return annotations(MarshalledApplyAnnotation.create(on).value(MarshalledAnnotation.create().dictionary(values).build()).build());
1891      }
1892
1893      /**
1894       * Beans don't require at least one property.
1895       *
1896       * <p>
1897       * When enabled, then a Java class doesn't need to contain at least 1 property to be considered a bean.
1898       * Otherwise, the bean will be serialized as a string using the {@link Object#toString()} method.
1899       *
1900       * <p>
1901       * The {@link Bean @Marshalled} annotation can be used on a class to override this setting when <jk>true</jk>.
1902       *
1903       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1904       * <p class='bjava'>
1905       *    <jc>// A bean with no properties.</jc>
1906       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1907       *    }
1908       *
1909       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that serializes beans even if they have zero properties.</jc>
1910       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
1911       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1912       *       .disableBeansRequireSomeProperties()
1913       *       .build();
1914       *
1915       *    <jc>// Produces:  {}</jc>
1916       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1917       * </p>
1918       *
1919       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1920       *    <li class='note'>The {@link Bean @Marshalled} annotation can be used on the class to force it to be recognized as a bean class
1921       *       even if it has no properties.
1922       * </ul>
1923       *
1924       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1925       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableBeansRequireSomeProperties()}
1926       * </ul>
1927       *
1928       * @return This object.
1929       */
1930      public Builder disableBeansRequireSomeProperties() {
1931         return disableBeansRequireSomeProperties(true);
1932      }
1933
1934      /**
1935       * Same as {@link #disableBeansRequireSomeProperties()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
1936       *
1937       * @param value The value for this setting.
1938       * @return This object.
1939       */
1940      public Builder disableBeansRequireSomeProperties(boolean value) {
1941         disableBeansRequireSomeProperties = value;
1942         return this;
1943      }
1944
1945      /**
1946       * Don't silently ignore missing setters.
1947       *
1948       * <p>
1949       * When enabled, trying to set a value on a bean property without a setter will throw a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
1950       * Otherwise, it will be silently ignored.
1951       *
1952       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1953       * <p class='bjava'>
1954       *    <jc>// A bean with a property with a getter but not a setter.</jc>
1955       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1956       *       <jk>public void</jk> getFoo() {
1957       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"foo"</js>;
1958       *       }
1959       *    }
1960       *
1961       *    <jc>// Create a parser that throws an exception if a setter is not found but a getter is.</jc>
1962       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
1963       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1964       *       .disableIgnoreMissingSetters()
1965       *       .build();
1966       *
1967       *    <jc>// Throws a ParseException.</jc>
1968       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
1969       * </p>
1970       *
1971       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1972       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on getters and fields to ignore them.
1973       * </ul>
1974       *
1975       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1976       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableIgnoreMissingSetters()}
1977       * </ul>
1978       *
1979       * @return This object.
1980       */
1981      public Builder disableIgnoreMissingSetters() {
1982         return disableIgnoreMissingSetters(true);
1983      }
1984
1985      /**
1986       * Same as {@link #disableIgnoreMissingSetters()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
1987       *
1988       * @param value The value for this setting.
1989       * @return This object.
1990       */
1991      public Builder disableIgnoreMissingSetters(boolean value) {
1992         disableIgnoreMissingSetters = value;
1993         return this;
1994      }
1995
1996      /**
1997       * Don't ignore transient fields.
1998       *
1999       * <p>
2000       * When enabled, methods and fields marked as <jk>transient</jk> will not be ignored as bean properties.
2001       *
2002       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2003       * <p class='bjava'>
2004       *    <jc>// A bean with a transient field.</jc>
2005       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2006       *       <jk>public transient</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>;
2007       *    }
2008       *
2009       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that doesn't ignore transient fields.</jc>
2010       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2011       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2012       *       .disableIgnoreTransientFields()
2013       *       .build();
2014       *
2015       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo":"foo"}</jc>
2016       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2017       * </p>
2018       *
2019       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2020       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used on transient fields to keep them from being ignored.
2021       * </ul>
2022       *
2023       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2024       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableIgnoreTransientFields()}
2025       * </ul>
2026       *
2027       * @return This object.
2028       */
2029      public Builder disableIgnoreTransientFields() {
2030         return disableIgnoreTransientFields(true);
2031      }
2032
2033      /**
2034       * Same as {@link #disableIgnoreTransientFields()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2035       *
2036       * @param value The value for this setting.
2037       * @return This object.
2038       */
2039      public Builder disableIgnoreTransientFields(boolean value) {
2040         disableIgnoreTransientFields = value;
2041         return this;
2042      }
2043
2044      /**
2045       * Don't ignore unknown properties with null values.
2046       *
2047       * <p>
2048       * When enabled, trying to set a <jk>null</jk> value on a non-existent bean property will throw a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
2049       * Otherwise it will be silently ignored.
2050       *
2051       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2052       * <p class='bjava'>
2053       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2054       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2055       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>;
2056       *    }
2057       *
2058       *    <jc>// Create a parser that throws an exception on an unknown property even if the value being set is null.</jc>
2059       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2060       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2061       *       .disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()
2062       *       .build();
2063       *
2064       *    <jc>// Throws a BeanRuntimeException wrapped in a ParseException on the unknown 'bar' property.</jc>
2065       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:null}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2066       * </p>
2067       *
2068       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2069       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()}
2070       * </ul>
2071       *
2072       * @return This object.
2073       */
2074      public Builder disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties() {
2075         return disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties(true);
2076      }
2077
2078      /**
2079       * Same as {@link #disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2080       *
2081       * @param value The value for this setting.
2082       * @return This object.
2083       */
2084      public Builder disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties(boolean value) {
2085         disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties = value;
2086         return this;
2087      }
2088
2089      /**
2090       * Don't use interface proxies.
2091       *
2092       * <p>
2093       * When enabled, interfaces will be instantiated as proxy classes through the use of an
2094       * {@link InvocationHandler} if there is no other way of instantiating them.
2095       * Otherwise, throws a {@link BeanRuntimeException}.
2096       *
2097       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2098       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#disableInterfaceProxies()}
2099       * </ul>
2100       *
2101       * @return This object.
2102       */
2103      public Builder disableInterfaceProxies() {
2104         return disableInterfaceProxies(true);
2105      }
2106
2107      /**
2108       * Same as {@link #disableInterfaceProxies()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2109       *
2110       * @param value The value for this setting.
2111       * @return This object.
2112       */
2113      public Builder disableInterfaceProxies(boolean value) {
2114         disableInterfaceProxies = value;
2115         return this;
2116      }
2117
2118      /**
2119       * POJO example.
2120       *
2121       * <p>
2122       * Specifies an example in JSON of the specified class.
2123       *
2124       * <p>
2125       * Examples are used in cases such as POJO examples in Swagger documents.
2126       *
2127       * <p>
2128       * Setting applies to specified class and all subclasses.
2129       *
2130       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2131       * <p class='bjava'>
2132       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
2133       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2134       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2135       *       .example(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>)
2136       *       .build();
2137       * </p>
2138       *
2139       * <p>
2140       * This is a shorthand method for the following code:
2141       * <p class='bjava'>
2142       *    <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>pojoClass</jv>).example(<jv>json</jv>).build())
2143       * </p>
2144       *
2145       * <p>
2146       * POJO examples can also be defined on classes via the following:
2147       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
2148       *    <li>A static field annotated with {@link Example @Example}.
2149       *    <li>A static method annotated with {@link Example @Example} with zero arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
2150       *    <li>A static method with name <c>example</c> with no arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
2151       * </ul>
2152       *
2153       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2154       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#example()}
2155       * </ul>
2156       *
2157       * @param <T> The POJO class type.
2158       * @param pojoClass The POJO class.
2159       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2160       * @param json The JSON 5 representation of the example.
2161       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty (treated as no example).
2162       * @return This object.
2163       */
2164      public <T> Builder example(Class<T> pojoClass, String json) {
2165         return annotations(MarshalledApplyAnnotation.create(assertArgNotNull(ARG_pojoClass, pojoClass)).value(MarshalledAnnotation.create().example(json).build()).build());
2166      }
2167
2168      /**
2169       * POJO example.
2170       *
2171       * <p>
2172       * Specifies an example of the specified class.
2173       *
2174       * <p>
2175       * Examples are used in cases such as POJO examples in Swagger documents.
2176       *
2177       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2178       * <p class='bjava'>
2179       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that excludes the 'foo' and 'bar' properties on the MyBean class.</jc>
2180       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2181       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2182       *       .example(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean().setFoo(<js>"foo"</js>).setBar(123))
2183       *       .build();
2184       * </p>
2185       *
2186       * <p>
2187       * This is a shorthand method for the following code:
2188       * <p class='bjava'>
2189       *       <jv>builder</jv>.annotations(MarshalledAnnotation.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>pojoClass</jv>).example(Json5.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.toString(<jv>object</jv>)).build())
2190       * </p>
2191       *
2192       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2193       *    <li class='note'>Using this method assumes the serialized form of the object is the same as that produced
2194       *       by the default serializer.  This may not be true based on settings or swaps on the constructed serializer.
2195       * </ul>
2196       *
2197       * <p>
2198       * POJO examples can also be defined on classes via the following:
2199       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
2200       *    <li>The {@link Marshalled#example()} annotation on the class itself.
2201       *    <li>A static field annotated with {@link Example @Example}.
2202       *    <li>A static method annotated with {@link Example @Example} with zero arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
2203       *    <li>A static method with name <c>example</c> with no arguments or one {@link MarshallingSession} argument.
2204       * </ul>
2205       *
2206       * @param <T> The POJO class.
2207       * @param pojoClass The POJO class.
2208       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2209       * @param o
2210       *    An instance of the POJO class used for examples.
2211       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will be serialized as <js>"null"</js>).
2212       * @return This object.
2213       */
2214      public <T> Builder example(Class<T> pojoClass, T o) {
2215         return annotations(MarshalledApplyAnnotation.create(assertArgNotNull(ARG_pojoClass, pojoClass)).value(MarshalledAnnotation.create().example(Json5.of(o)).build()).build());
2216      }
2217
2218      /**
2219       * Find fluent setters.
2220       *
2221       * <p>
2222       * When enabled, fluent setters are detected on beans during parsing.
2223       *
2224       * <p>
2225       * Fluent setters must have the following attributes:
2226       * <ul>
2227       *    <li>Public.
2228       *    <li>Not static.
2229       *    <li>Take in one parameter.
2230       *    <li>Return the bean itself.
2231       * </ul>
2232       *
2233       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2234       * <p class='bjava'>
2235       *    <jc>// A bean with a fluent setter.</jc>
2236       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2237       *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean foo(String <jv>value</jv>) {...}
2238       *    }
2239       *
2240       *    <jc>// Create a parser that finds fluent setters.</jc>
2241       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2242       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2243       *       .findFluentSetters()
2244       *       .build();
2245       *
2246       *    <jc>// Parse into bean using fluent setter.</jc>
2247       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>);
2248       * </p>
2249       *
2250       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2251       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation can also be used on methods to individually identify them as fluent setters.
2252       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#findFluentSetters() @BeanType.findFluentSetters()} annotation can also be used on classes to specify to look for fluent setters.
2253       * </ul>
2254       *
2255       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2256       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#findFluentSetters()}
2257       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#findFluentSetters()}
2258       * </ul>
2259       *
2260       * @return This object.
2261       */
2262      public Builder findFluentSetters() {
2263         return findFluentSetters(true);
2264      }
2265
2266      /**
2267       * Same as {@link #findFluentSetters()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2268       *
2269       * @param value The value for this setting.
2270       * @return This object.
2271       */
2272      public Builder findFluentSetters(boolean value) {
2273         findFluentSetters = value;
2274         return this;
2275      }
2276
2277      /**
2278       * Find fluent setters.
2279       *
2280       * <p>
2281       * Identical to {@link #findFluentSetters()} but enables it on a specific class only.
2282       *
2283       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2284       * <p class='bjava'>
2285       *    <jc>// A bean with a fluent setter.</jc>
2286       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2287       *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean foo(String <jv>value</jv>) {...}
2288       *    }
2289       *
2290       *    <jc>// Create a parser that finds fluent setters.</jc>
2291       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2292       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2293       *       .findFluentSetters(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
2294       *       .build();
2295       *
2296       *    <jc>// Parse into bean using fluent setter.</jc>
2297       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>);
2298       * </p>
2299       *
2300       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2301       *    <li class='note'>This method is functionally equivalent to using the {@link BeanType#findFluentSetters()} annotation.
2302       * </ul>
2303       *
2304       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2305       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#findFluentSetters()}
2306       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #findFluentSetters()}
2307       * </ul>
2308       *
2309       * @param on The class that this applies to.
2310       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2311       * @return This object.
2312       */
2313      public Builder findFluentSetters(Class<?> on) {
2314         assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
2315         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(on).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().findFluentSetters(true).build()).build());
2316      }
2317
2318      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
2319      public HashKey hashKey() {
2320         // @formatter:off
2321         return HashKey.of(
2322            super.hashKey(),
2323            beanClassVisibility,
2324            beanConstructorVisibility,
2325            beanMethodVisibility,
2326            beanFieldVisibility,
2327            beanDictionary,
2328            swaps,
2329            notBeanClasses,
2330            notBeanPackages,
2331         integer(
2332            disableBeansRequireSomeProperties,
2333            beanMapPutReturnsOldValue,
2334            beansRequireDefaultConstructor,
2335            beansRequireSerializable,
2336            beansRequireSettersForGetters,
2337            disableIgnoreTransientFields,
2338            disableIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties,
2339            disableIgnoreMissingSetters,
2340            disableInterfaceProxies,
2341            findFluentSetters,
2342            ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters,
2343            ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters,
2344            ignoreUnknownBeanProperties,
2345            ignoreUnknownEnumValues,
2346            unsortedProperties,
2347            useJavaBeanIntrospector,
2348            validateSchema,
2349            disableDefaultViewInclusion
2350         ),
2351         activeView,
2352            typePropertyName,
2353            mediaType,
2354            timeZone,
2355            durationFormat,
2356            periodFormat,
2357            calendarFormat,
2358            dateFormat,
2359            temporalFormat,
2360            timeZoneFormat,
2361            localeFormat,
2362            binaryFormat,
2363            enumFormat,
2364            uuidFormat,
2365            bitSetFormat,
2366            bigNumberFormat,
2367            booleanFormat,
2368            floatFormat,
2369            currencyFormat,
2370            classFormat,
2371            locale,
2372            propertyNamer,
2373            System.identityHashCode(beanStore),
2374            System.identityHashCode(classLoader)
2375         );
2376         // @formatter:on
2377      }
2378
2379      /**
2380       * Sets the default active view name for all sessions created from this context.
2381       *
2382       * <p>
2383       * When set, only bean properties whose declared {@link MarshalledProp#view()} set contains this view name
2384       * will be included during serialization.  On the parse side, properties outside the active view are routed
2385       * through the existing unknown/ignored-property mechanism governed by
2386       * {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}.
2387       *
2388       * <p>
2389       * Untagged properties (those whose declared view set is empty) follow the default-view-inclusion policy:
2390       * included under every active view unless {@link #disableDefaultViewInclusion()} has been called.
2391       *
2392       * <p>
2393       * This value can be overridden per serialization or parse call via the session builder (e.g.
2394       * {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#activeView(String)}).
2395       *
2396       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2397       * <p class='bjava'>
2398       *    JsonSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer.<jsm>create</jsm>()
2399       *       .activeView(<js>"summary"</js>)
2400       *       .build();
2401       * </p>
2402       *
2403       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2404       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledProp#view()}
2405       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#activeView(String)}
2406       * </ul>
2407       *
2408       * @param value The active view name.  Use <jk>null</jk> to disable view filtering.
2409       * @return This object.
2410       * @since 10.0.0
2411       */
2412      public Builder activeView(String value) {
2413         activeView = value;
2414         return this;
2415      }
2416
2417      /**
2418       * Disables the default-view-inclusion policy.
2419       *
2420       * <p>
2421       * By default, bean properties that carry no {@link MarshalledProp#view()} declaration are included under
2422       * every active view (matching Jackson's {@code DEFAULT_VIEW_INCLUSION} behavior).
2423       * Calling this method reverses the policy: untagged properties are <em>excluded</em> when any active view
2424       * is set.
2425       *
2426       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2427       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledProp#view()}
2428       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #activeView(String)}
2429       * </ul>
2430       *
2431       * @return This object.
2432       * @since 10.0.0
2433       */
2434      public Builder disableDefaultViewInclusion() {
2435         return disableDefaultViewInclusion(true);
2436      }
2437
2438      /**
2439       * Same as {@link #disableDefaultViewInclusion()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2440       *
2441       * @param value The value for this setting.
2442       * @return This object.
2443       * @since 10.0.0
2444       */
2445      public Builder disableDefaultViewInclusion(boolean value) {
2446         disableDefaultViewInclusion = value;
2447         return this;
2448      }
2449
2450      /**
2451       * Ignore invocation errors on getters.
2452       *
2453       * <p>
2454       * When enabled, errors thrown when calling bean getter methods will silently be ignored.
2455       * Otherwise, a {@code BeanRuntimeException} is thrown.
2456       *
2457       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2458       * <p class='bjava'>
2459       *    <jc>// A bean with a property that throws an exception.</jc>
2460       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2461       *       <jk>public</jk> String getFoo() {
2462       *          <jk>throw new</jk> RuntimeException(<js>"foo"</js>);
2463       *       }
2464       *    }
2465       *
2466       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores bean getter exceptions.</jc>
2467       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2468       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2469       *       .ingoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()
2470       *       .build();
2471       *
2472       *    <jc>// Exception is ignored.</jc>
2473       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2474       * </p>
2475       *
2476       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2477       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()}
2478       * </ul>
2479       *
2480       * @return This object.
2481       */
2482      public Builder ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters() {
2483         return ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters(true);
2484      }
2485
2486      /**
2487       * Same as {@link #ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2488       *
2489       * @param value The value for this setting.
2490       * @return This object.
2491       */
2492      public Builder ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters(boolean value) {
2493         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters = value;
2494         return this;
2495      }
2496
2497      /**
2498       * Ignore invocation errors on setters.
2499       *
2500       * <p>
2501       * When enabled, errors thrown when calling bean setter methods will silently be ignored.
2502       * Otherwise, a {@code BeanRuntimeException} is thrown.
2503       *
2504       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2505       * <p class='bjava'>
2506       *    <jc>// A bean with a property that throws an exception.</jc>
2507       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2508       *       <jk>public void</jk> setFoo(String <jv>foo</jv>) {
2509       *          <jk>throw new</jk> RuntimeException(<js>"foo"</js>);
2510       *       }
2511       *    }
2512       *
2513       *    <jc>// Create a parser that ignores bean setter exceptions.</jc>
2514       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2515       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2516       *       .ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()
2517       *       .build();
2518       *
2519       *    <jc>// Exception is ignored.</jc>
2520       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2521       * </p>
2522       *
2523       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2524       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()}
2525       * </ul>
2526       *
2527       * @return This object.
2528       */
2529      public Builder ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters() {
2530         return ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters(true);
2531      }
2532
2533      /**
2534       * Same as {@link #ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2535       *
2536       * @param value The value for this setting.
2537       * @return This object.
2538       */
2539      public Builder ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters(boolean value) {
2540         ignoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters = value;
2541         return this;
2542      }
2543
2544      /**
2545       * Ignore unknown properties.
2546       *
2547       * <p>
2548       * When enabled, trying to set a value on a non-existent bean property will silently be ignored.
2549       * Otherwise, a {@code BeanRuntimeException} is thrown.
2550       *
2551       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2552       * <p class='bjava'>
2553       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2554       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2555       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf>;
2556       *    }
2557       *
2558       *    <jc>// Create a parser that ignores missing bean properties.</jc>
2559       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2560       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2561       *       .ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()
2562       *       .build();
2563       *
2564       *    <jc>// Doesn't throw an exception on unknown 'bar' property.</jc>
2565       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"{foo:'foo',bar:'bar'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2566       * </p>
2567       *
2568       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2569       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}
2570       * </ul>
2571       *
2572       * @return This object.
2573       */
2574      public Builder ignoreUnknownBeanProperties() {
2575         return ignoreUnknownBeanProperties(true);
2576      }
2577
2578      /**
2579       * Same as {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2580       *
2581       * @param value The value for this setting.
2582       * @return This object.
2583       */
2584      public Builder ignoreUnknownBeanProperties(boolean value) {
2585         ignoreUnknownBeanProperties = value;
2586         return this;
2587      }
2588
2589      /**
2590       * Schema validation mode.
2591       *
2592       * <p>
2593       * When enabled, bean property values are validated against the constraints declared by
2594       * {@link Schema @Schema} annotations on those properties.  Validation runs
2595       * during both <b>parsing</b> (value set on the bean) and <b>serialization</b> (value read from the bean).
2596       *
2597       * <p>
2598       * Backed by the typed {@code JsonSchema} bean in {@code juneau-bean-jsonschema} and JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
2599       * semantics.  Validation failures throw {@link SchemaValidationException}
2600       * wrapped in {@code BeanRuntimeException}; the parser surfaces them as
2601       * {@link ParseException} and the serializer surfaces them as
2602       * {@link SerializeException}.
2603       *
2604       * <p>
2605       * If {@code juneau-bean-jsonschema} is not on the classpath, this flag becomes a silent no-op.
2606       *
2607       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2608       * <p class='bjava'>
2609       *    <jc>// A bean with a length-constrained property.</jc>
2610       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2611       *       <ja>&#64;Schema</ja>(minLength=<js>"2"</js>, maxLength=<js>"10"</js>)
2612       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>name</jf>;
2613       *    }
2614       *
2615       *    <jc>// Parser that validates inbound values.</jc>
2616       *    ReaderParser <jv>p</jv> = JsonParser.<jsm>create</jsm>().validateSchema().build();
2617       *
2618       *    <jv>p</jv>.parse(<js>"{name:'a'}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// throws ParseException - too short</jc>
2619       * </p>
2620       *
2621       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2622       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#validateSchema()}
2623       * </ul>
2624       *
2625       * @return This object.
2626       * @since 10.0.0
2627       */
2628      public Builder validateSchema() {
2629         return validateSchema(true);
2630      }
2631
2632      /**
2633       * Same as {@link #validateSchema()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2634       *
2635       * @param value The value for this setting.
2636       * @return This object.
2637       * @since 10.0.0
2638       */
2639      public Builder validateSchema(boolean value) {
2640         validateSchema = value;
2641         return this;
2642      }
2643
2644      /**
2645       * Ignore unknown properties.
2646       *
2647       * <p>
2648       * When enabled, unknown enum values will be set to <jk>null</jk> instead of throwing an exception.
2649       *
2650       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2651       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#ignoreUnknownEnumValues()}
2652       * </ul>
2653       *
2654       * @return This object.
2655       */
2656      public Builder ignoreUnknownEnumValues() {
2657         return ignoreUnknownEnumValues(true);
2658      }
2659
2660      /**
2661       * Same as {@link #ignoreUnknownEnumValues()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
2662       *
2663       * @param value The value for this setting.
2664       * @return This object.
2665       */
2666      public Builder ignoreUnknownEnumValues(boolean value) {
2667         ignoreUnknownEnumValues = value;
2668         return this;
2669      }
2670
2671      /**
2672       * Implementation classes.
2673       *
2674       * <p>
2675       * For interfaces and abstract classes this method can be used to specify an implementation class for the
2676       * interface/abstract class so that instances of the implementation class are used when instantiated (e.g. during a
2677       * parse).
2678       *
2679       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2680       * <p class='bjava'>
2681       *    <jc>// A bean interface.</jc>
2682       *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyBean {
2683       *       ...
2684       *    }
2685       *
2686       *    <jc>// A bean implementation.</jc>
2687       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanImpl <jk>implements</jk> MyBean {
2688       *       ...
2689       *    }
2690
2691       *    <jc>// Create a parser that instantiates MyBeanImpls when parsing MyBeans.</jc>
2692       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2693       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2694       *       .implClass(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, MyBeanImpl.<jk>class</jk>)
2695       *       .build();
2696       *
2697       *    <jc>// Instantiates a MyBeanImpl,</jc>
2698       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"..."</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2699       * </p>
2700       *
2701       * @param interfaceClass The interface class.
2702       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2703       * @param implClass The implementation class.
2704       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2705       * @return This object.
2706       */
2707      public Builder implClass(Class<?> interfaceClass, Class<?> implClass) {
2708         assertArgNotNull(ARG_interfaceClass, interfaceClass);
2709         assertArgNotNull(ARG_implClass, implClass);
2710         return annotations(MarshalledApplyAnnotation.create(interfaceClass).value(MarshalledAnnotation.create().implClass(implClass).build()).build());
2711      }
2712
2713      /**
2714       * Implementation classes.
2715       *
2716       * <p>
2717       * For interfaces and abstract classes this method can be used to specify an implementation class for the
2718       * interface/abstract class so that instances of the implementation class are used when instantiated (e.g. during a
2719       * parse).
2720       *
2721       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2722       * <p class='bjava'>
2723       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2724       *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyBean {
2725       *       ...
2726       *    }
2727       *
2728       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2729       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanImpl <jk>implements</jk> MyBean {
2730       *       ...
2731       *    }
2732
2733       *    <jc>// Create a parser that instantiates MyBeanImpls when parsing MyBeans.</jc>
2734       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
2735       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2736       *       .implClasses(AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, MyBeanImpl.<jk>class</jk>))
2737       *       .build();
2738       *
2739       *    <jc>// Instantiates a MyBeanImpl,</jc>
2740       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"..."</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2741       * </p>
2742       *
2743       * @param values
2744       *    The new value for this setting.
2745       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2746       * @return This object.
2747       */
2748      public Builder implClasses(Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> values) {
2749         assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
2750         values.forEach((k, v) -> annotations(MarshalledApplyAnnotation.create(k).value(MarshalledAnnotation.create().implClass(v).build()).build()));
2751         return this;
2752      }
2753
2754      /**
2755       * Identifies a class to be used as the interface class for the specified class and all subclasses.
2756       *
2757       * <p>
2758       * When specified, only the list of properties defined on the interface class will be used during serialization.
2759       * Additional properties on subclasses will be ignored.
2760       *
2761       * <p class='bjava'>
2762       *    <jc>// Parent class or interface</jc>
2763       *    <jk>public abstract class</jk> A {
2764       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>;
2765       *    }
2766       *
2767       *    <jc>// Sub class</jc>
2768       *    <jk>public class</jk> A1 <jk>extends</jk> A {
2769       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
2770       *    }
2771       *
2772       *    <jc>// Create a serializer and define our interface class mapping.</jc>
2773       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2774       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2775       *       .interfaceClass(A1.<jk>class</jk>, A.<jk>class</jk>)
2776       *       .build();
2777       *
2778       *    <jc>// Produces "{"foo":"foo"}"</jc>
2779       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> A1());
2780       * </p>
2781       *
2782       * <p>
2783       * This annotation can be used on the parent class so that it filters to all child classes, or can be set
2784       * individually on the child classes.
2785       *
2786       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2787       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#interfaceClass() @BeanType(interfaceClass)} annotation is the equivalent annotation-based solution.
2788       * </ul>
2789       *
2790       * @param on The class that the interface class applies to.
2791       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2792       * @param value
2793       *    The new value for this setting.
2794       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2795       * @return This object.
2796       */
2797      public Builder interfaceClass(Class<?> on, Class<?> value) {
2798         assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
2799         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
2800         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(on).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().interfaceClass(value).build()).build());
2801      }
2802
2803      /**
2804       * Identifies a set of interfaces.
2805       *
2806       * <p>
2807       * When specified, only the list of properties defined on the interface class will be used during serialization
2808       * of implementation classes.  Additional properties on subclasses will be ignored.
2809       *
2810       * <p class='bjava'>
2811       *    <jc>// Parent class or interface</jc>
2812       *    <jk>public abstract class</jk> A {
2813       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"foo"</js>;
2814       *    }
2815       *
2816       *    <jc>// Sub class</jc>
2817       *    <jk>public class</jk> A1 <jk>extends</jk> A {
2818       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>bar</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
2819       *    }
2820       *
2821       *    <jc>// Create a serializer and define our interface class mapping.</jc>
2822       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2823       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2824       *       .interfaces(A.<jk>class</jk>)
2825       *       .build();
2826       *
2827       *    <jc>// Produces "{"foo":"foo"}"</jc>
2828       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> A1());
2829       * </p>
2830       *
2831       * <p>
2832       * This annotation can be used on the parent class so that it filters to all child classes, or can be set
2833       * individually on the child classes.
2834       *
2835       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2836       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#interfaceClass() @BeanType(interfaceClass)} annotation is the equivalent annotation-based solution.
2837       * </ul>
2838       *
2839       * @param value
2840       *    The new value for this setting.
2841       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2842       * @return This object.
2843       */
2844      public Builder interfaces(Class<?>...value) {
2845         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_value, value);
2846         for (var v : value)
2847            annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(v).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().interfaceClass(v).build()).build());
2848         return this;
2849      }
2850
2851      /**
2852       * <i><l>Context</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Locale.
2853       *
2854       * <p>
2855       * Specifies the default locale for serializer and parser sessions when not specified via {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#locale(Locale)}.
2856       * Typically used for POJO swaps that need to deal with locales such as swaps that convert <l>Date</l> and <l>Calendar</l>
2857       * objects to strings by accessing it via the session passed into the {@link ObjectSwap#swap(MarshallingSession, Object)} and
2858       * {@link ObjectSwap#unswap(MarshallingSession, Object, ClassMeta, String)} methods.
2859       *
2860       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2861       * <p class='bjava'>
2862       *    <jc>// Define a POJO swap that skips serializing beans if we're in the UK.</jc>
2863       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanSwap <jk>extends</jk> StringSwap&lt;MyBean&gt; {
2864       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2865       *       <jk>public</jk> String swap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2866       *          <jk>if</jk> (<jv>session</jv>.getLocale().equals(Locale.<jsf>UK</jsf>))
2867       *             <jk>return null</jk>;
2868       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>bean</jv>.toString();
2869       *       }
2870       *    }
2871       *
2872       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses the specified locale if it's not passed in through session args.</jc>
2873       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2874       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2875       *       .locale(Locale.<jsf>UK</jsf>)
2876       *       .swaps(MyBeanSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
2877       *       .build();
2878       * </p>
2879       *
2880       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2881       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#locale()}
2882       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#locale(Locale)}
2883       * </ul>
2884       *
2885       * @param value The new value for this property.
2886       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2887       * @return This object.
2888       */
2889      public Builder locale(Locale value) {
2890         locale = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
2891         return this;
2892      }
2893
2894      /**
2895       * <i><l>Context</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Media type.
2896       *
2897       * <p>
2898       * Specifies the default media type for serializer and parser sessions when not specified via {@link MarshallingSession.Builder#mediaType(MediaType)}.
2899       * Typically used for POJO swaps that need to serialize the same POJO classes differently depending on
2900       * the specific requested media type.   For example, a swap could handle a request for media types <js>"application/json"</js>
2901       * and <js>"application/json+foo"</js> slightly differently even though they're both being handled by the same JSON
2902       * serializer or parser.
2903       *
2904       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2905       * <p class='bjava'>
2906       *    <jc>// Define a POJO swap that skips serializing beans if the media type is application/json.</jc>
2907       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBeanSwap <jk>extends</jk> StringSwap&lt;MyBean&gt; {
2908       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2909       *       <jk>public</jk> String swap(MarshallingSession <jv>session</jv>, MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2910       *          <jk>if</jk> (<jv>session</jv>.getMediaType().equals(<js>"application/json"</js>))
2911       *             <jk>return null</jk>;
2912       *          <jk>return</jk> <jv>bean</jv>.toString();
2913       *       }
2914       *    }
2915       *
2916       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses the specified media type if it's not passed in through session args.</jc>
2917       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2918       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2919       *       .mediaType(MediaType.<jsf>JSON</jsf>)
2920       *       .build();
2921       * </p>
2922       *
2923       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2924       *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#mediaType()}
2925       *    <li class='jm'>{@link MarshallingSession.Builder#mediaType(MediaType)}
2926       * </ul>
2927       *
2928       * @param value The new value for this property.
2929       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no default media type will be set).
2930       * @return This object.
2931       */
2932      public Builder mediaType(MediaType value) {
2933         mediaType = value;
2934         return this;
2935      }
2936
2937      /**
2938       * Returns the set of not-bean classes.
2939       *
2940       * <p>
2941       * Gives access to the inner set if you need to make more than simple additions via {@link #notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...)}.
2942       *
2943       * @return The set of not-bean classes.
2944       * @see #notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...)
2945       */
2946      public Set<ClassInfo> notBeanClasses() {
2947         return notBeanClasses;
2948      }
2949
2950      /**
2951       * Convenience method for {@link #notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...)} that accepts {@link Class} objects.
2952       *
2953       * @param values
2954       *    The values to add to this setting.
2955       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
2956       * @return This object.
2957       * @see #notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...)
2958       */
2959      public Builder notBeanClasses(Class<?>...values) {
2960         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
2961         return notBeanClasses(Stream.of(values).map(x -> info(x)).toArray(ClassInfo[]::new));
2962      }
2963
2964      /**
2965       * Bean class exclusions.
2966       *
2967       * <p>
2968       * List of classes that should not be treated as beans even if they appear to be bean-like.
2969       * Not-bean classes are converted to <c>Strings</c> during serialization.
2970       *
2971       * <p>
2972       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
2973       * <ul>
2974       *    <li>Classes.
2975       *    <li>Arrays and collections of classes.
2976       * </ul>
2977       *
2978       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2979       * <p class='bjava'>
2980       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
2981       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2982       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
2983       *
2984       *       <jk>public</jk> String toString() {
2985       *          <jk>return</jk> <js>"baz"</js>;
2986       *       }
2987       *    }
2988       *
2989       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that doesn't treat MyBean as a bean class.</jc>
2990       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
2991       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2992       *       .notBeanClasses(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>)
2993       *       .build();
2994       *
2995       *    <jc>// Produces "baz" instead of {"foo":"bar"}</jc>
2996       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
2997       * </p>
2998       *
2999       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3000       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} annotation can also be used on classes to prevent them from being recognized as beans.
3001       * </ul>
3002       *
3003       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3004       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanIgnore}
3005       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanConfig#notBeanClasses()}
3006       * </ul>
3007       *
3008       * @param values
3009       *    The values to add to this setting.
3010       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
3011       * @return This object.
3012       */
3013      public Builder notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...values) {
3014         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
3015         notBeanClasses().addAll(l(values));
3016         return this;
3017      }
3018
3019      /**
3020       * Same as {@link #notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...)} but allows you to pass in a collection of class info objects.
3021       *
3022       * @param values
3023       *    The values to add to this setting.
3024       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> values.
3025       * @return This object.
3026       * @see #notBeanClasses(ClassInfo...)
3027       */
3028      public Builder notBeanClasses(Collection<ClassInfo> values) {
3029         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
3030         notBeanClasses().addAll(values);
3031         return this;
3032      }
3033
3034      /**
3035       * Returns the list of not-bean Java package names.
3036       *
3037       * <p>
3038       * Gives access to the inner list if you need to make more than simple additions via {@link #notBeanPackages(String...)}.
3039       *
3040       * @return The list of not-bean Java package names.
3041       * @see #notBeanPackages(String...)
3042       */
3043      public Set<String> notBeanPackages() {
3044         return notBeanPackages;
3045      }
3046
3047      /**
3048       * Same as {@link #notBeanPackages(String...)} but allows you to pass in a collection of classes.
3049       *
3050       * @param values
3051       *    The values to add to this setting.
3052       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> values.
3053       * @return This object.
3054       * @see #notBeanPackages(String...)
3055       */
3056      public Builder notBeanPackages(Collection<String> values) {
3057         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
3058         notBeanPackages().addAll(values);
3059         return this;
3060      }
3061
3062      /**
3063       * Bean package exclusions.
3064       *
3065       * <p>
3066       * Used as a convenient way of defining the {@link #notBeanClasses(Class...)} property for entire packages.
3067       * Any classes within these packages will be serialized to strings using {@link Object#toString()}.
3068       *
3069       * <p>
3070       * Note that you can specify suffix patterns to include all subpackages.
3071       *
3072       * <p>
3073       * Values can consist of any of the following types:
3074       * <ul>
3075       *    <li>Strings.
3076       *    <li>Arrays and collections of strings.
3077       * </ul>
3078       *
3079       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3080       * <p class='bjava'>
3081       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that ignores beans in the specified packages.</jc>
3082       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3083       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3084       *       .notBeanPackages(<js>"org.apache.foo"</js>, <js>"org.apache.bar.*"</js>)
3085       *       .build();
3086       * </p>
3087       *
3088       * @param values
3089       *    The values to add to this setting.
3090       *    <br>Values can consist of any of the following types:
3091       *    <ul>
3092       *       <li>{@link Package} objects.
3093       *       <li>Strings.
3094       *       <li>Arrays and collections of anything in this list.
3095       *    </ul>
3096       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
3097       * @return This object.
3098       */
3099      public Builder notBeanPackages(String...values) {
3100         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
3101         return notBeanPackages(l(values));
3102      }
3103
3104      /**
3105       * Bean property namer
3106       *
3107       * <p>
3108       * Same as {@link #propertyNamer(Class)} but allows you to specify a namer for a specific class.
3109       *
3110       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3111       * <p class='bjava'>
3112       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
3113       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
3114       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>fooBarBaz</jf> = <js>"fooBarBaz"</js>;
3115       *    }
3116       *
3117       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses Dashed-Lower-Case property names for the MyBean class only.</jc>
3118       *    <jc>// (e.g. "foo-bar-baz" instead of "fooBarBaz")</jc>
3119       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3120       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3121       *       .propertyNamer(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>, PropertyNamerDLC.<jk>class</jk>)
3122       *       .build();
3123       *
3124       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo-bar-baz":"fooBarBaz"}</jc>
3125       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
3126       * </p>
3127       *
3128       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3129       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#propertyNamer() @BeanType(propertyNamer)}
3130       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #propertyNamer(Class)}
3131       * </ul>
3132       *
3133       * @param on The class that the namer applies to.
3134       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3135       * @param value
3136       *    The new value for this setting.
3137       *    <br>The default is {@link BasicPropertyNamer}.
3138       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3139       * @return This object.
3140       */
3141      public Builder propertyNamer(Class<?> on, Class<? extends PropertyNamer> value) {
3142         assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
3143         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
3144         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(on).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().propertyNamer(value).build()).build());
3145      }
3146
3147      /**
3148       * Bean property namer
3149       *
3150       * <p>
3151       * The class to use for calculating bean property names.
3152       *
3153       * <p>
3154       * Predefined classes:
3155       * <ul>
3156       *    <li>{@link BasicPropertyNamer} - Default.
3157       *    <li>{@link PropertyNamerDLC} - Dashed-lower-case names.
3158       *    <li>{@link PropertyNamerULC} - Dashed-upper-case names.
3159       * </ul>
3160       *
3161       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3162       * <p class='bjava'>
3163       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property.</jc>
3164       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
3165       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>fooBarBaz</jf> = <js>"fooBarBaz"</js>;
3166       *    }
3167       *
3168       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that uses Dashed-Lower-Case property names.</jc>
3169       *    <jc>// (e.g. "foo-bar-baz" instead of "fooBarBaz")</jc>
3170       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3171       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3172       *       .propertyNamer(PropertyNamerDLC.<jk>class</jk>)
3173       *       .build();
3174       *
3175       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"foo-bar-baz":"fooBarBaz"}</jc>
3176       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
3177       * </p>
3178       *
3179       * @param value
3180       *    The new value for this setting.
3181       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use {@link BasicPropertyNamer} as the default).
3182       * @return This object.
3183       */
3184      public Builder propertyNamer(Class<? extends PropertyNamer> value) {
3185         propertyNamer = value;
3186         return this;
3187      }
3188
3189      /**
3190       * Disable sorted bean properties.
3191       *
3192       * <p>
3193       * By default, all bean properties are serialized and accessed in alphabetical order.
3194       * Calling this method opts out of that behavior so that the natural JVM order is used instead.
3195       *
3196       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3197       * <p class='bjava'>
3198       *    <jc>// A bean with 3 properties.</jc>
3199       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
3200       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>c</jf> = <js>"1"</js>;
3201       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>b</jf> = <js>"2"</js>;
3202       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>a</jf> = <js>"3"</js>;
3203       *    }
3204       *
3205       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that does NOT sort bean properties.</jc>
3206       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3207       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3208       *       .unsortedProperties()
3209       *       .build();
3210       *
3211       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"c":"1","b":"2","a":"3"} (JVM order)</jc>
3212       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> MyBean());
3213       * </p>
3214       *
3215       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3216       *    <li class='note'>The {@link BeanType#unsorted() @BeanType.unsorted()} annotation can also be used to opt out of sorting on a single class.
3217       * </ul>
3218       *
3219       * @return This object.
3220       */
3221      public Builder unsortedProperties() {
3222         return unsortedProperties(true);
3223      }
3224
3225      /**
3226       * Same as {@link #unsortedProperties()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
3227       *
3228       * @param value The value for this setting.
3229       * @return This object.
3230       */
3231      public Builder unsortedProperties(boolean value) {
3232         unsortedProperties = value;
3233         return this;
3234      }
3235
3236      /**
3237       * Opt specific bean classes out of alphabetical property sorting.
3238       *
3239       * <p>
3240       * Applies the {@link BeanType#unsorted() @BeanType(unsorted=true)} annotation to the specified classes,
3241       * overriding the default sorted behavior for those specific beans.
3242       *
3243       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3244       *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanType#unsorted() @BeanType(unsorted)}
3245       * </ul>
3246       *
3247       * @param on The bean classes to opt out of sorted properties.
3248       *    <br>Cannot contain <jk>null</jk> values.
3249       * @return This object.
3250       */
3251      public Builder unsortedProperties(Class<?>...on) {
3252         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_on, on);
3253         for (var c : on)
3254            annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(c).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().unsorted(true).build()).build());
3255         return this;
3256      }
3257
3258      /**
3259       * Identifies a stop class for the annotated class.
3260       *
3261       * <p>
3262       * Identical in purpose to the stop class specified by {@link Introspector#getBeanInfo(Class, Class)}.
3263       * Any properties in the stop class or in its base classes will be ignored during analysis.
3264       *
3265       * <p>
3266       * For example, in the following class hierarchy, instances of <c>C3</c> will include property <c>p3</c>,
3267       * but not <c>p1</c> or <c>p2</c>.
3268       *
3269       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3270       * <p class='bjava'>
3271       *    <jk>public class</jk> C1 {
3272       *       <jk>public int</jk> getP1();
3273       *    }
3274       *
3275       *    <jk>public class</jk> C2 <jk>extends</jk> C1 {
3276       *       <jk>public int</jk> getP2();
3277       *    }
3278       *
3279       *    <jk>public class</jk> C3 <jk>extends</jk> C2 {
3280       *       <jk>public int</jk> getP3();
3281       *    }
3282       *
3283       *    <jc>// Create a serializer specifies a stop class for C3.</jc>
3284       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3285       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3286       *       .stopClass(C3.<jk>class</jk>, C2.<jk>class</jk>)
3287       *       .build();
3288       *
3289       *    <jc>// Produces:  {"p3":"..."}</jc>
3290       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <jv>serializer</jv>.serialize(<jk>new</jk> C3());
3291       * </p>
3292       *
3293       * @param on The class on which the stop class is being applied.
3294       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3295       * @param value
3296       *    The new value for this setting.
3297       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3298       * @return This object.
3299       */
3300      public Builder stopClass(Class<?> on, Class<?> value) {
3301         assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
3302         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
3303         return annotations(BeanTypeApplyAnnotation.create(on).value(BeanTypeAnnotation.create().stopClass(value).build()).build());
3304      }
3305
3306      /**
3307       * A shortcut for defining a {@link FunctionalSwap}.
3308       *
3309       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3310       * <p class='bjava'>
3311       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that performs a custom format for DAte objects.</jc>
3312       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3313       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3314       *       .swap(Date.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jsm>format</jsm>(<jv>x</jv>))
3315       *       .build();
3316       * </p>
3317       *
3318       * @param <T> The object type being swapped out.
3319       * @param <S> The object type being swapped in.
3320       * @param normalClass The object type being swapped out.
3321       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3322       * @param swappedClass The object type being swapped in.
3323       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3324       * @param swapFunction The function to convert the object.
3325       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3326       * @return This object.
3327       */
3328      public <T,S> Builder swap(Class<T> normalClass, Class<S> swappedClass, ThrowingFunction<T,S> swapFunction) {
3329         return swap(normalClass, swappedClass, swapFunction, null);
3330      }
3331
3332      /**
3333       * A shortcut for defining a {@link FunctionalSwap}.
3334       *
3335       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3336       * <p class='bjava'>
3337       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that performs a custom format for Date objects.</jc>
3338       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
3339       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3340       *       .swap(Date.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jsm>format</jsm>(<jv>x</jv>), <jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jsm>parse</jsm>(<jv>x</jv>))
3341       *       .build();
3342       * </p>
3343       *
3344       * @param <T> The object type being swapped out.
3345       * @param <S> The object type being swapped in.
3346       * @param normalClass The object type being swapped out.
3347       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3348       * @param swappedClass The object type being swapped in.
3349       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3350       * @param swapFunction The function to convert the object during serialization.
3351       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3352       * @param unswapFunction The function to convert the object during parsing.
3353       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3354       * @return This object.
3355       */
3356      public <T,S> Builder swap(Class<T> normalClass, Class<S> swappedClass, ThrowingFunction<T,S> swapFunction, ThrowingFunction<S,T> unswapFunction) {
3357         assertArgNotNull(ARG_normalClass, normalClass);
3358         assertArgNotNull(ARG_swappedClass, swappedClass);
3359         assertArgNotNull(ARG_swapFunction, swapFunction);
3360         assertArgNotNull(ARG_unswapFunction, unswapFunction);
3361         swaps().add(0, new FunctionalSwap<>(normalClass, swappedClass, swapFunction, unswapFunction));
3362         return this;
3363      }
3364
3365      /**
3366       * Returns the bean swaps list.
3367       *
3368       * <p>
3369       * Gives access to the inner list if you need to make more than simple additions via {@link #swaps(Class...)}.
3370       *
3371       * @return The bean swaps list.
3372       * @see #swaps(Class...)
3373       */
3374      public List<Object> swaps() {
3375         return swaps;
3376      }
3377
3378      /**
3379       * Same as {@link #swaps(Object...)} but explicitly specifies an array of classes to avoid compilation warnings.
3380       *
3381       * @param values
3382       *    The values to add to this setting.
3383       *    <br>Values can consist of any of the following types:
3384       *    <ul>
3385       *       <li>Any subclass of {@link ObjectSwap}.
3386       *       <li>Any surrogate class.  A shortcut for defining a {@link SurrogateSwap}.
3387       *    </ul>
3388       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3389       * @return This object.
3390       */
3391      public Builder swaps(Class<?>...values) {
3392         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_values, values);
3393         swaps().addAll(0, accumulate((Object[])values));
3394         return this;
3395      }
3396
3397      /**
3398       * Java object swaps.
3399       *
3400       * <p>
3401       * Swaps are used to "swap out" non-serializable classes with serializable equivalents during serialization,
3402       * and "swap in" the non-serializable class during parsing.
3403       *
3404       * <p>
3405       * An example of a swap would be a <c>Calendar</c> object that gets swapped out for an ISO8601 string.
3406       *
3407       * <p>
3408       * Multiple swaps can be associated with a single class.
3409       * When multiple swaps are applicable to the same class, the media type pattern defined by
